subclassFluff.name: Nightstalker
entries: {@i Become an Assassin of the Unholy Trinity}

Nightstalkers are the ruthless executioners of the darkest pantheons. Whether fueled by the strife of Cyric, the twisting influence of Mask, or the festering hate of Moander, the Nightstalker is a walking herald of the "Crawling Abyss." They are not mere murderers; they are zealous instruments of the unseen. Striking from the ceiling or erupting from the floorboards, they drag their blades through the choking dark to deliver the perfect, terrifying blow.

subclassFluff.name: Marauder
entries: {@i Become a Battlefield Executioner}

While some fighters rely on the elegance of a blade or the precision of an arrow, the Marauder relies on the brutal physics of the clinch. These warriors are specialists in close-quarters lockdown, trained to exploit every gap in an opponent's posture to drag them to the dirt or squeeze the life from their lungs. Often found as elite urban enforcers, pit fighters, or shock troopers, Marauders treat the battlefield like a wrestling mat where they are the undisputed masters.

subclassFluff.name: Fate Domain
entries: {@i Look Into the Weave and See a Thousand Futures}

Gods of fate perceive the future not as a single thread, but as a sprawling tapestry of choices that drive mortals toward their destinies. Some deities consider the end preordained, while others understand the multiverse as a chaotic web of infinite possibility. Clerics who draw power from the forces of fate are weavers of this web. They receive fleeting omens, pluck the strings of probability to share impossible knowledge with their allies, and prophesize their enemies' inevitable doom.

subclassFluff.name: Vestige Patron
entries: {@i Wield the Remnants of a Dying God’s Power}

Your pact draws on the power of a dying god, a being once worshipped by countless followers but now reduced to an infant, an amnesiac, or a helpless avatar. This dying god, known as a vestige, might have been benevolent, evil, or strange and unknowable, but right now, it is entirely dependent on you. It needs your help to survive, learn about the world, and eventually regain its former power. In exchange, it shares its erratic, overwhelming strength with you—provided you can keep it in a good mood.

subclassFluff.name: Path of Wild Magic
entries: Many places in the multiverse abound with beauty, intense emotion, and rampant magic; the Feywild, the Upper Planes, and other realms of supernatural power radiate with such forces and can profoundly influence people. As folk of deep feeling, barbarians are especially susceptible to these wild influences, with some barbarians being transformed by the magic. These magic-suffused barbarians walk the Path of Wild Magic. Elf, tiefling, aasimar, and genasi barbarians often seek this path, eager to manifest the otherworldly magic of their ancestors.

subclassFluff.name: Path of the Juggernaut
entries: Juggernaut barbarians are siege engines in their own right, able to stand their ground and move their foes into positions of weakness. While raging, they make any battlefield their domain, shoving foes over mountainsides and destroying even the strongest structures with their attacks.

subclassFluff.name: The Seeker
entries: {@i Plumb the depths of the multiverse for forgotten knowledge.}

While some warlocks make pacts with fiends or fey, you have bound yourself to the pursuit of pure, unfiltered knowledge. You are a {@b Seeker}, a cosmic wanderer entrusted by an unfathomable patron to explore the furthest reaches of the Astral Plane and beyond. Your patron might be a deity of knowledge like Oghma or Celestian, a cosmic entity dwelling in the Far Realm, or an ancient mortal who transcended the bounds of reality.

Many famous Seekers have left their mark on the multiverse. The legendary witch Tasha was once a Seeker, serving as an apprentice to an ancient Netherese archmage known only as {@i The Archivist}. When the Netherese empire fell, The Archivist used immense magic to teleport their grand observatory into the timeless void of the Astral Sea, where they now act as a patron, granting ambitious mortals a fraction of their power in exchange for the secrets they uncover in the material world.

subclassFluff.name: Sea Sorcery
entries: {@i Your Power Drawn from the Deep}

Your innate magic comes from the vast and untamable power of the sea. You might be the descendant of a sea god, have been saved from drowning by a powerful elemental, or perhaps you were born in a storm so mighty it imprinted its magic upon your soul. Whatever its origin, your magic is the magic of the crashing wave, the dark pressure of the abyss, and the sudden, shocking strike of a storm. You are a conduit for the ocean's beautiful and terrifying power.

subclassFluff.name: Order Domain
entries: {@i Use Law to Subjugate and Delegate}

The Order Domain represents discipline, as well as devotion to the laws that govern a society, an institution, or a philosophy. Clerics of Order meditate on logic and justice as they serve their gods.

Clerics of Order believe that well-crafted laws establish legitimate hierarchies, and those selected by law to lead must be obeyed. Those who obey must do so to the best of their ability, and if those who lead fail to protect the law, they must be replaced. In this manner, law weaves a web of obligations that create order and security in a chaotic multiverse.

subclassFluff.name: Grave Knight
entries: {@i Walking the Line Between Life and Death}

A Grave Knight is a warrior who has tethered their soul to the grim energies of the necropolis. They are not necessarily evil, but they understand that death is a tool, a source of power, and an inevitable part of the cycle. Drawing power from this connection, a Grave Knight wreathes their blade in necrotic energy, teleports through shadowy paths, and channels the chilling touch of the beyond.

subclassFluff.name: Grave Knight
entries: As a Magic Action, you can become a conduit for the grave's power for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have Resistance to Necrotic and Poison damage.
You have Advantage on saving throws against being Frightened or Paralyzed.
Your melee weapon attacks deal an additional 2d6 Necrotic damage.
When you use your {@i Grave Step} feature, you can make one melee weapon attack against a target within your reach as part of that same Bonus Action.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFluff.name: Path of the Beast
entries: {@i Transform to Gain Savage Bestial Traits}
Barbarians who walk the Path of the Beast draw their rage from a bestial spark burning within their souls. That beast bursts forth in the throes of rage, physically transforming the barbarian.
Such a barbarian might be inhabited by a primal spirit or be descended from shape-shifters. You can choose the origin of your feral might or determine it by rolling on the Origin of the Beast table.
{@b Origin of the Beast}

rows: One of your parents is a lycanthrope, and you've inherited some of their curse.
You are descended from an archdruid and inherited the ability to partially change shape.
A fey spirit gifted you with the ability to adopt different bestial aspects.
An ancient animal spirit dwells within you, allowing you to walk this path.

subclassFluff.name: Path of the Giant
entries: {@i Channel the Stature and Power of Giants}

Barbarians who walk the Path of the Giant draw strength from the same primal forces as giants. As they rage, these barbarians surge with elemental power and grow in size, taking on forms that evoke the glory of giants. Some barbarians look like oversized versions of themselves, perhaps with a hint of elemental energy flaring in their eyes and around their weapons. Others transform more dramatically, taking on the appearance of an actual giant or a form similar to an Elemental, wreathed in fire, frost, or lightning.

subclassFluff.name: College of Creation
entries: {@i Use the World’s Song to manifest your own Creations}

Bards believe the cosmos is a work of art—the creation of the first dragons and gods. That creative work included harmonies that continue to resound through existence today, a power known as the Song of Creation. The bards of the College of Creation draw on that primeval song through dance, music, and poetry, and their teachers share this lesson: “Before the sun and the moon, there was the Song, and its music awoke the first dawn. Its melodies so delighted the stones and trees that some of them gained a voice of their own. And now they sing too. Learn the Song, students, and you too can teach the mountains to sing and dance.”

Dwarves and gnomes often encourage their bards to become students of the Song of Creation. And among dragonborn, the Song of Creation is revered, for legends portray Bahamut and Tiamat—the greatest of dragons—as two of the song’s first singers.

subclassFluff.name: College of Eloquence
entries: {@i Command Mastery over the Spoken Word}

Adherents of the College of Eloquence master the art of oratory. Persuasion is regarded as a high art, and a well-reasoned, well-spoken argument often proves more persuasive than facts. These bards wield a blend of logic and theatrical wordplay, winning over skeptics and detractors with logical arguments and plucking at heartstrings to appeal to the emotions of audiences.

subclassFluff.name: College of Swords
entries: {@i Turn Combat Mastery into a Performance}

Bards of the College of Swords are called blades, and they entertain through daring feats of weapon prowess. Blades perform stunts such as sword swallowing, knife throwing and juggling, and mock combats. Though they use their weapons to entertain, they are also highly trained and skilled warriors in their own right.

Their talent with weapons inspires many blades to lead double lives. One blade might use a circus troupe as cover for nefarious deeds such as assassination, robbery, and blackmail. Other blades strike at the wicked, bringing justice to bear against the cruel and powerful. Most troupes are happy to accept a blade’s talent for the excitement it adds to a performance, but few entertainers fully trust a blade in their ranks.

Blades who abandon their lives as entertainers have often run into trouble that makes maintaining their secret activities impossible. A blade caught stealing or engaging in vigilante justice is too great a liability for most troupes. With their weapon skills and magic, these blades either take up work as enforcers for thieves’ guilds or strike out on their own as adventurers.

subclassFluff.name: College of Whispers
entries: {@i Inspire Paranoia and Use Social Sabotage}

Most folk are happy to welcome a bard into their midst. Bards of the College of Whispers use this to their advantage. They appear to be like other bards, sharing news, singing songs, and telling tales to the audiences they gather. In truth, the College of Whispers teaches its students that they are wolves among sheep. These bards use their knowledge and magic to uncover secrets and turn them against others through extortion and threats.

Many other Bards hate the College of Whispers, viewing it as a parasite that uses a bard’s reputation to acquire wealth and power. For this reason, members of this college rarely reveal their true nature. They typically claim to follow some other college, or they keep their actual calling secret in order to infiltrate and exploit royal courts and other settings of power.

subclassFluff.name: Death Domain
entries: {@i Reap the Souls of the Unworthy Living}

The Death domain is concerned with the forces that cause death, as well as the negative energy that gives rise to undead creatures. Deities such as Chemosh, Myrkul, and Wee Jas are patrons of necromancers, death knights, liches, mummy lords, and vampires. Gods of the Death domain also embody murder (Anubis, Bhaal, and Pyremius), pain (Iuz or Loviatar), disease or poison (Incabulos, Talona, or Morgion), and the underworld (Hades and Hel).

subclassFluff.name: Forge Domain
entries: {@i Create your Divine Masterwork}

The gods of the forge are patrons of artisans who work with metal, from a humble blacksmith who keeps a village in horseshoes and plow blades to the mighty elf artisan whose diamond-tipped arrows of mithral have felled demon lords. The gods of the forge teach that, with patience and hard work, even the most intractable metal can be transformed from a lump of ore to a beautifully wrought object. Clerics of these deities search for objects lost to the forces of darkness, liberate mines overrun by orcs, and uncover rare and wondrous materials necessary to create potent magic items. Followers of these gods take great pride in their work, and they are willing to craft and use heavy armor and powerful weapons to protect them. Deities of this domain include Gond, Reorx, Onatar, Moradin, Hephaestus, and Goibhniu.

subclassFluff.name: Nature Domain
entries: {@i Protect the Natural World and Harness its Power}

Gods of nature are as varied as the natural world itself; from inscrutable gods of the deep forests (such as Silvanus, Obad-Hai, Chislev, Balinor, and Pan) to friendly deities associated with particular springs and groves (such as Eldath). Druids revere nature as a whole and might serve one of these deities, practicing mysterious rites and reciting all-but-forgotten prayers in their own secret tongue. But many of these gods have clerics as well, champions who take a more active role in advancing the interests of a particular nature god. These clerics might hunt the evil monstrosities that despoil the woodlands, bless the harvest of the faithful, or wither the crops of those who anger their gods.

subclassFluff.name: Peace Domain
entries: {@i Foster Friendliness and Form Bonds}

The balm of peace thrives at the heart of healthy communities, between friendly nations, and in the souls of the kindhearted. The gods of peace inspire people of all sorts to resolve conflict and to stand up against those forces that try to prevent peace from flourishing. See the Peace Deities table for a list of some of the gods associated with this domain.

Clerics of the Peace Domain preside over the signing of treaties, and they are often asked to arbitrate in disputes. These clerics' blessings draw people together and help them shoulder one another's burdens, and the clerics' magic aids those who are driven to fight for the way of peace.
##### Peace Deities

subclassFluff.name: Tempest Domain
entries: {@i Smite Foes with the Wrath of the Sky}

Gods whose portfolios include the Tempest domain – including Talos, Umberlee, Kord, Zeboim, the Devourer, Zeus, and Thor – govern storms, sea, and, sky. They include gods of lightning and thunder, gods of earthquakes, some fire gods, and certain gods of violence, physical strength, and courage. In some pantheons, a god of this domain rules over other deities and is known for swift justice delivered by thunderbolts. In the pantheons of seafaring people, gods of this domain are ocean deities and the patrons of sailors. Tempest gods send their clerics to inspire fear in the common folk, either to keep those folk on the path of righteousness or to encourage them to offer sacrifices of propitiation to ward off divine wrath.

subclassFluff.name: Twilight Domain
entries: {@i Defend Allies with the Soothing Twilight}

The twilit transition from light into darkness often brings calm and even joy, as the day's labors end and the hours of rest begin. The darkness can also bring terrors, but the gods of twilight guard against the horrors of the night.

Clerics who serve these deities-examples of which appear on the Twilight Deities table-bring comfort to those who seek rest and protect them by venturing into the encroaching darkness to ensure that the dark is a comfort, not a terror.
##### Twilight Deities

subclassFluff.name: Circle of Dreams
entries: {@i Survive the Wilds with the Protection of the Fey Courts}

Druids who are members of the Circle of Dreams hail from regions that have strong ties to the Feywild and its dreamlike realms. The Druids’ guardianship of the natural world makes for a natural alliance between them and good-aligned fey. These Druids seek to fill the world with dreamy wonder. Their magic mends wounds and brings joy to downcast hearts, and the realms they protect are gleaming, fruitful places, where dream and reality blur together and where the weary can find rest.

subclassFluff.name: Circle of the Shepherd
entries: {@i Call Nature Spirits to your Aid}

Druids of the Circle of the Shepherd commune with the spirits of nature, especially the spirits of beasts and the fey, and call to those spirits for aid. These druids recognize that all living things play a role in the natural world, yet they focus on protecting animals and fey creatures that have difficulty defending themselves. Shepherds, as they are known, see such creatures as their charges. They ward off monsters that threaten them, rebuke hunters who kill more prey than necessary, and prevent civilization from encroaching on rare animal habitats and on sites sacred to the fey. Many of these Druids are happiest far from cities and towns, content to spend their days in the company of animals and the fey creatures of the wilds.

Members of this circle become adventurers to oppose forces that threaten their charges or to seek knowledge and power that will help them safeguard their charges better. Wherever these Druids go, the spirits of the wilderness are with them.

subclassFluff.name: Circle of Spores
entries: {@i Embrace the Cycle of  Life and Death}
Druids of the Circle of Spores find beauty in decay. They see within mold and other fungi the ability to transform lifeless material into abundant, albeit somewhat strange, life.

These druids believe that life and death are parts of a grand cycle, with one leading to the other and then back again. Death isn’t the end of life, but instead a change of state that sees life shift into a new form.

Druids of this circle have a complex relationship with the undead. Unlike most other Druids, they see nothing inherently wrong with undeath, which they consider to be a companion to life and death. But these Druids believe that the natural cycle is healthiest when each segment of it is vibrant and changing. Undead that seek to replace all life with undeath, or that try to avoid passing to a final rest, violate the cycle and must be thwarted.

subclassFluff.name: Circle of Wildfire
entries: {@i Embrace the Destructive and Restorative Powers of Fire}

Druids within the Circle of Wildfire understand that destruction is sometimes the precursor of creation, such as when a forest fire promotes later growth. These Druids bond with a primal spirit that harbors both destructive and creative power, allowing the druids to create controlled flames that burn away one thing but give life to another.

subclassFluff.name: Echo Knight
entries: {@i Conjure a Mirrored Self from Alternate Timelines}

A mysterious and feared frontline warrior of the Kryn Dynasty, the Echo Knight has mastered the art of using dunamis to summon the fading shades of unrealized timelines to aid them in battle. Surrounded by echoes of their own might, they charge into the fray as a cycling swarm of shadows and strikes.

subclassFluff.name: Rune Knight
entries: {@i Channel the Runic Magic of Giants}

Rune Knights enhance their martial prowess using the supernatural power of runes, an ancient practice that originated with giants. Rune cutters can be found among any family of giants, and you likely learned your methods first or second hand from such a mystical artisan. Whether you found the giant’s work carved into a hill or cave, learned of the runes from a sage, or met the giant in person, you studied the giant’s craft and learned how to apply magic runes to empower your equipment.

subclassFluff.name: Samurai
entries: {@i Fight Deliberately and Precisely. Be Unwavering}

The Samurai is a fighter who draws on an implacable fighting spirit to overcome enemies. A samurai’s resolve is nearly unbreakable, and the enemies in a samurai’s path have two choices: yield or die fighting.

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of the Ascendant Dragon
entries: {@i Harness your Inner Draconic Power}

The fundamental teaching of this tradition holds that by emulating dragons, a monk becomes a more integrated part of the world and its magic. By altering their spirit to resonate with draconic might, monks who follow this tradition augment their prowess in battle, bolster their allies, and can even soar through the air on draconic wings. But all this power is in service of a greater goal: achieving a spiritual unity with the essence of the Material Plane.
As a follower of this Monastic Tradition, you decide how you unlocked the power of dragons through your focus. The Ascendant Dragon Origin table offers some possibilities.
##### Ascendant Dragon Origin

rows: You honed your abilities by observing a dragon and aligning your ki with their world altering power.
A dragon personally took an active role in shaping your inner energy.
You studied at a monastery that traces its teachings back centuries or more to a single dragon’s instruction.
You spent long stretches meditating in the region of influence of an ancient dragon’s lair, absorbing its ambient magic.
You found a scroll written in Draconic that contained inspiring new techniques.
After a dream that featured a five-handed dragonborn you awoke with altered ki, reflecting the breaths of dragons.

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of the Astral Self
entries: {@i Strike your Enemies with Four Arms at Once}

A monk who follows the Way of the Astral Self believes their body is an illusion. They see their ki as a representation of their true form, an astral self. This astral self has the capacity to be a force of order or disorder, with some monasteries training students to use their power to protect the weak and other instructing aspirants in how to manifest their true selves in service to the mighty.

subclassFluff.name: Kensei Warrior
entries: {@i Masterfully Mix Weapons with Martial Arts}

Monks of the Way of the Kensei train relentlessly with their weapons, to the point that the weapon becomes like an extension of the body. Founded on a mastery of sword fighting, the tradition has expanded to include many different weapons.

A kensei sees a weapon much in the same way a calligrapher or a painter regards a pen or brush. Whatever the weapon, the kensei views it as a tool used to express the beauty and precision of the martial arts. That such mastery makes a kensei a peerless warrior is but a side effect of intense devotion, practice, and study.

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of the Long Death
entries: {@i Master Death to Become Unkillable}

Monks of the Way of the Long Death are obsessed with the meaning and mechanics of dying. They capture creatures and prepare elaborate experiments to capture, record, and understand the moments of their demise. They then use this knowledge to guide their understanding of martial arts, yielding a deadly fighting style.

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of the Sun Soul
entries: {@i Blast Foes from a Distance with Radiant Energy}

Monks of the Way of the Sun Soul learn to channel their own life energy into searing bolts of light. They teach that meditation can unlock the ability to unleash the indomitable light shed by the soul of every living creature.

subclassFluff.name: Oath of Conquest
entries: {@i Subjugate the Unworthy with an Iron Fist}

The Oath of Conquest calls to paladins who seek glory in battle and the subjugation of their enemies. It isn’t enough for these paladins to establish order. They must crush the forces of chaos. Sometimes called knight tyrants or iron mongers, those who swear this oath gather into grim orders that serve gods or philosophies of war and well-ordered might.

Some of these paladins go so far as to consort with the powers of the Nine Hells, valuing the rule of law over the balm of mercy. The archdevil Bel, warlord of Avernus, counts many of these paladins – called hell knights – as his most ardent supporters. Hell knights cover their armor with trophies taken from fallen enemies, a grim warning to any who dare oppose them and the decrees of their lords. These knights are often most fiercely resisted by other paladins of this oath, who believe that the hell knights have wandered too far into darkness.
These Paladins share the following tenets:

items: Your victory must be overwhelming, douse the flames of hope.
Tolerate no dissent. Your word is law.
Never be complacent in your rule, for forces will rise to challenge it.

subclassFluff.name: Oath of the Crown
entries: {@i Defend Civilization and Law from Tyranny}

The Oath of the Crown is sworn to the ideals of civilization, be it the spirit of a nation, fealty to a sovereign, or service to a deity of law and rulership. The paladins who swear this oath dedicate themselves to serving society and, in particular, the just laws that hold society together. These paladins are the watchful guardians on the walls, standing against the chaotic tides of barbarism that threaten to tear down all that civilization has built, and are commonly known as guardians, exemplars, or sentinels. Often, paladins who swear this oath are members of an order of knighthood in service to a nation or a sovereign, and undergo their oath as part of their admission to the order's ranks.
These Paladins share the following tenets:

items: Respect and uphold the law.
Stay true to those you are loyal to.
Have courage to face threats, even against overwhelming odds.
Take responsibility for the consequences of your actions.

subclassFluff.name: Oath of Redemption
entries: {@i Help Sway the Lost Toward a Lighter Path}

The Oath of Redemption sets a paladin on a difficult path, one that requires a holy warrior to use violence only as a last resort. Paladins who dedicate themselves to this oath believe that any person can be redeemed and that the path of benevolence and justice is one that anyone can walk. These paladins face evil creatures in the hope of turning their foes to the light, and they slay their enemies only when such a deed will clearly save other lives. Paladins who follow this path are known as redeemers.

While redeemers are idealists, they are no fools. Redeemers know that undead, demons, devils, and other supernatural threats can be inherently evil. Against such foes, paladins who swear this oath bring the full wrath of their weapons and spells to bear. Yet the redeemers still pray that, one day, even creatures of wickedness will invite their own redemption.
These Paladins share the following tenets:

items: Always strive to solve conflicts with diplomacy.
Seek to redeem those that have been corrupted.
Have patience, for true change takes time.
Keep your heart and mind clear, and show wisdom in the face of hard decisions.

subclassFluff.name: Oath of the Watchers
entries: {@i Defend the Material Plane from Invading Forces}

The Oath of the Watchers binds Paladins to protect mortal realms from the predations of extraplanar creatures, many of which can lay waste to mortal soldiers. Thus, the Watchers hone their minds, spirits, and bodies to be the ultimate weapons against such threats.

Paladins who follow the Watchers’ oath are ever vigilant in spotting the influence of extraplanar forces, often establishing a network of spies and informants to gather information on suspected cults. To a Watcher, keeping a healthy suspicion and awareness about one’s surroundings is as natural as wearing armor in battle.
These Paladins share the following tenets:

items: Be ever alert for the corruption of invading forces.
Never accept gifts or favors from fiends or those who truck with them.
Keep your blade sharp and your mind keen with strict discipline

subclassFluff.name: Drakewarden
entries: {@i Bond with a Draconic Companion}

Your connection to the natural world takes the form of a draconic spirit, which can manifest in physical form as a drake. As your powers grow, your drake grows as well, blossoming from a small four-legged companion to a majestic winged creature large and strong enough for you to ride. Along the way, you gain an increasing share of the awe-inspiring power of dragons.

subclassFluff.name: Horizon Walker
entries: {@i Wander through Portals and Gates}

Horizon walkers guard the world against threats that originate from other planes or that seek to ravage the mortal realm with otherworldly magic. They seek out planar portals and keep watch over them, venturing to the Inner Planes and the Outer Planes as needed to pursue their foes. These rangers are also friends to any forces in the multiverse – especially benevolent dragons, fey, and elementals – that work to preserve life and the order of the planes.

subclassFluff.name: Monster Slayer
entries: {@i Study and Slay Monsters of All Variety}

You have dedicated yourself to hunting down creatures of the night and wielders of grim magic. A Monster Slayer seeks out vampires, dragons, evil fey, fiends, and other magical threats. Trained in supernatural techniques to overcome such monsters, slayers are experts at unearthing and defeating mighty, mystical foes.

subclassFluff.name: Swarmkeeper
entries: {@i Bond with a Swarm of Beasts or Spirits}

Feeling a deep connection to the environment around them, some rangers reach out through their magical connection to the world and bond with a swarm of nature spirits. The swarm becomes a potent force in battle, as well as helpful company for the ranger. Some Swarmkeepers are outcasts or hermits, keeping to themselves and their attendant swarms rather than dealing with the discomfort of others. Other Swarmkeepers enjoy building vibrant communities that work for the mutual benefit of all those they consider part of their swarm.

subclassFluff.name: Inquisitive
entries: {@i Search for Clues and Solve Mysteries}

As an archetypal Inquisitive, you excel at rooting out secrets and unraveling mysteries. You rely on your sharp eye for detail, but also on your finely honed ability to read the words and deeds of other creatures to determine their true intent. You excel at defeating creatures that hide among and prey upon ordinary folk, and your mastery of lore and your sharp eye make you well equipped to expose and end hidden evils.

subclassFluff.name: Mastermind
entries: {@i Outsmart Opponents with Masterful Tactics}

Your focus is on people and on the influence and secrets they have. Many spies, courtiers, and schemers follow this archetype, leading lives of intrigue. Words are your weapons as often as knives or poison, and secrets and favors are some of your favorite treasures.

subclassFluff.name: Scout
entries: {@i Skirmish with Speed and Skill}

You are skilled in stealth and surviving far from the streets of a city, allowing you to scout ahead of your companions during expeditions. Rogues who embrace this archetype are at home in the wilderness and among barbarians and rangers, and many Scouts serve as the eyes and ears of war bands. Ambusher, spy, bounty hunter — these are just a few of the roles that Scouts assume as they range the world.

subclassFluff.name: Swashbuckler
entries: {@i Excel in Single Combat with Brash Confidence}

As a Swashbuckler, you focus on the art of the blade, bringing speed, elegance and charm to your combat style \- topped off with an exquisite flourish of panache. You excel in single combat, and your methods make a duel look like a flamboyant performance.

subclassFluff.name: Divine Sorcery
entries: {@i Channel Magic of the Upper Planes}

Sometimes the spark of magic that fuels a Sorcerer comes from a divine source that glimmers within the soul. Having such a blessed soul is a sign that your innate magic might come from a distant but powerful familial connection to a divine being. Perhaps your ancestor was an angel, transformed into a mortal and sent to fight in a god’s name. Or your birth might align with an ancient prophecy, marking you as a servant of the gods or a chosen vessel of divine magic.

A Divine Sorcerer, with a natural magnetism, is seen as a threat by some religious hierarchies. As an outsider who commands sacred power, a Divine Sorcerer can undermine an existing order by claiming a direct tie to the divine.

In some cultures, only those who can claim the power of a Divine Sorcerer may command religious power. In these lands, ecclesiastical positions are dominated by a few bloodlines and preserved over generations.

subclassFluff.name: Lunar Sorcery
entries: {@i Unleash the Radiant Power of the Moon}

On many worlds, the moon is a revered celestial body with magical properties. On Krynn, the gods of magic are associated with the world’s three moons. On the world of Toril, the god Selûne uses the light of the moon to battle darkness. On Eberron, scholars of the Draconic Prophecy decipher ancient secrets from the waxing and waning of that world’s twelve moons.

You or someone from your lineage has been exposed to the concentrated magic of the moon (or moons) of your world, imbuing you with lunar magic. Perhaps your ancestor was involved in a druidic ritual involving an eclipse, or maybe a mystical fragment of a moon crashed near you. However you came to have your magic, your connection to the moon is obvious when you cast sorcerer spells—perhaps making your pupils glow with the color of a moon from your world, causing spectral manifestations of lunar phases to orbit you, or some other effect.

subclassFluff.name: Storm Sorcery
entries: {@i Unleash the Crackling Storm Within}

Your innate magic comes from the power of elemental air. Many with this power can trace their magic back to a near-death experience caused by the Great Rain, but perhaps you were born during a howling gale so powerful that folk still tell stories of it, or your lineage might include the influence of potent air creatures such as vaati or djinn. Whatever the case, the magic of the storm permeates your being.

Storm sorcerers are invaluable members of a ship's crew. Their magic allows them to exert control over wind and weather in their immediate area. Their abilities also prove useful in repelling attacks by sahuagin, pirates, and other waterborne threats.

subclassFluff.name: Fathomless Patron
entries: {@i Draw Power from the Unknowable Oceanic Deeps}

You have plunged into a pact with the deeps. An entity of the ocean, the Elemental Plane of Water, or another otherworldly sea now allows you to draw on its thalassic power. Is it merely using you to learn about terrestrial realms, or does it want you to open cosmic floodgates and drown the world?

Perhaps you were born into a generational cult that venerates the Fathomless and its spawn. Or you might have been shipwrecked and on the brink of drowning when your patron’s grasp offered you a chance at life. Whatever the reason for your pact, the sea and its unknown depths call to you.
Entities of the deep that might empower a Warlock include krakens, ancient water elementals, godlike hallucinations dreamed into being by kuo-toa, merfolk demigods, and sea hag covens.

subclassFluff.name: Genie Patron
entries: {@i Negotiate Carefully with an Elemental Wish-Granter}

You have made a pact with one of the rarest kinds of genie, a noble genie. Such entities rule vast fiefs on the Elemental Planes and have great influence over lesser genies and elemental creatures. Noble genies are varied in their motivations, but most are arrogant and wield power that rivals that of lesser deities. They delight in turning the table on mortals, who often bind genies into servitude, and readily enter into pacts that expand their reach.
{@b Genie Kind}
You choose your patron’s kind or determine it randomly, using the Genie Kind table.

subclassFluff.name: Arcane Scribe
entries: {@i Demonstrate the True Power of the Word}

Magic of the book—that’s what many folk call wizardry. The name is apt, given how much time Wizards spend poring over tomes and penning theories about the nature of magic. It’s rare to see Wizards traveling without books and scrolls sprouting from their bags, and a Wizard would go to great lengths to plumb an archive of ancient knowledge.

Among Wizards, Arcane Scribes are the most bookish. While Arcane Scribes take many forms, their mission is consistent: recording magical discoveries so that wizardry can flourish. And while all Wizards value spellbooks, Scribes magically awaken their books, turning it into a trusted companion. All Wizards study books, but a wizardly scribe talks to theirs\!

subclassFluff.name: Bladesinger
entries: {@i Balance Sword and Spell with the Power of Song}

Bladesingers master a tradition of wizardry that incorporates swordplay and dance. Originally created by elves, this tradition has been adopted by non-elf practitioners, who honor and expand on the elven ways.

In combat, a bladesinger uses a series of intricate, elegant maneuvers that fend off harm and allow the bladesinger to channel magic into devastating attacks and a cunning defense. Many who have observed a bladesinger at work remember the display as one of the more beautiful experiences in their life, a glorious dance accompanied by a singing blade.

subclassFluff.name: Chronurgist
entries: {@i Exercise Influence over Time}

Focusing on the manipulation of time, those who follow the Chronurgy tradition learn to alter the pace of reality to their liking. Using the ramping of anticipatory dunamis energy, these mages can bend the flow of time as adroitly as a skilled musician plays an instrument, lending themselves and their allies an advantage in the blink of an eye.

subclassFluff.name: Graviturgist
entries: {@i Exert Mastery over the Laws of Attraction}

Understanding and mastering the forces that draw bodies of matter together or drive them apart, the students of the Graviturgy arcane tradition learn to further bend and manipulate the violent energy of gravity to their benefit, and the terrible detriment of their enemies.

subclassFluff.name: War Mage
entries: {@i Mix Protective and Destructive Magic to Master the Battlefield}

A variety of arcane colleges specialize in training Wizards for war. The tradition of War Magic blends principles of Evocation and Abjuration, rather than specializing in either of those schools. It teaches techniques that empower a caster’s spells, while also providing methods for wizards to bolster their own defenses.

Followers of this tradition are known as War Mages. They see their magic as both a weapon and armor, a resource superior to any piece of steel. War mages act fast in battle, using their spells to seize tactical control of a situation. Their spells strike hard, while their defensive skills foil their opponents’ attempts to counterattack. War Mages are also adept at turning other spellcasters’ magical energy against them.

In great battles, a War Mage often works with evokers, abjurers, and other types of wizards. Evokers, in particular, sometimes tease War Mages for splitting their attention between offense and defense. A war mage’s typical response: “What good is being able to throw a mighty fireball if I die before I can cast it?”

subclassFluff.name: War Mage
entries: Your Arcane Deflection becomes infused with deadly magic. When you use your Arcane Deflection feature, you can cause magical energy to arc from you. Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within 60 feet of you each take Force damage equal to half your Wizard level (rounded up).
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Level 2 features moved to level 3\.}
{@i Arcane Deflection’s benefits now last until the start of your next turn, and on your turn you can cast spells of level 1 or higher, but they can’t exceed a fifth of your wizard level.}
{@i Power Surge now lets you prepare Counterspell and Dispel Magic for free, and the extra damage now equals your full Wizard level.}
{@i You now gain a power surge when you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with a Wizard spell using a spell slot.}

subclassFluff.name: Circle of Preservation
entries: {@i Safeguard Nature and Heal the World}

subclassFluff.name: Defiled Sorcery
entries: {@i Siphon Life into Catastrophic Spells}

entries.name: Design Note: Hit Point Dice
entries: {@variantrule Monster|XPHB|Monsters}, like {@variantrule Player Character|XPHB|player characters}, have {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}. The size and number of a monster's {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} are typically denoted in the monster's {@variantrule stat block|XPHB} as part of the parenthetical after the monster's average {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Any creature—both {@variantrule Player Character|XPHB|characters} and {@variantrule Monster|XPHB|monsters}—can expend {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
When a {@variantrule monster|XPHb} is first encountered, assume that it hasn't expended any of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice}.

subclassFluff.name: Gladiator
entries: {@i Master Brutality and Blood Sport}

subclassFluff.name: Sorcerer-King Patron
entries: {@i Herald Tyrannical Might of Monstrous Rulers}

subclassFluff.name: Arcane Archer
entries: {@i Deploy Magical Effects through Enchanted Ammunition}

entries.name: Design Note: Arcane Archer Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@class Fighter|XPHB|Unearthed Arcana|Arcane Archer|XUA2025ArcaneSubclasses}:

items.name: Curving Shot
entries: has been revised to use the same ammunition but a new attack roll.

items.name: Magical Ammunition
entries: is a new feature that grants magical effects to your ammunition that can be used outside of combat.

items.name: Ever-Ready Shot
entries: is now available at a later level but always regains one expended Arcane Shot Die.

items.name: Arcane Burst
entries: is a new feature that grants an additional effect to {@classFeature Indomitable|Fighter|XPHB|9|XPHB}.

items.name: Masterful Shots
entries: has been redesigned and now allows you to reposition and attack.

subclassFluff.name: Tattooed Warrior
entries: {@i Enhance Martial Arts with Your Magic Tattoos}

entries.name: Design Note: Tattooed Warrior Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its last appearance in {@class Monk|XPHB|Unearthed Arcana|Tattooed Warrior|XUA2025ArcaneSubclasses}:

items: The Tattooed Warrior has been redesigned. With some exceptions, the tattoos now focus on additional abilities instead of spells.

items.name: Beast Tattoos
entries: still grant a cantrip, but now each option provides a benefit that isn't tied to a level 1 spell.

items.name: Celestial Tattoo
entries: has been redesigned to provide uses of Focus Points outside of combat.

items.name: Nature Tattoo
entries: has been condensed to provide Damage {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to a small suite of choices that can change.

items.name: Monster Tattoos
entries: has been redesigned with new options.

subclassFluff.name: College of Spirits
entries: {@i Call Forth Spirits from Beyond the Grave}

entries.name: Design Note: College of Spirits Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Van Richten's Guide to Ravenloft|VRGR}:

items.name: Guiding Whispers
entries: and {@b Spiritual Focus} are now combined into a single feature, {@subclassFeature Channeler|Bard|XPHB|Spirits|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}. This new feature also grants you proficiency with {@item playing cards|XPHB}.

items.name: Spirits from Beyond
entries: (formerly {@subclassFeature Tales from Beyond|Bard|PHB|Spirits|VRGR|3|VRGR}) has been reworked to improve its action economy and produce more immediate effects. The options on the feature's table have been revised as well.

items.name: Empowered Channeling
entries: is a new feature that allows the {@class Bard|XPHB} to cast and alter the {@spell Spirit Guardians|XPHB} spell.

subclassFluff.name: Grave Domain
entries: {@i Embody Deific Forces of Death}

entries.name: Design Note: Grave Domain Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Xanathar's Guide to Everything|XGE}:

items.name: Circle of Mortality
entries: now also grants you a small damage boost against {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creatures.

items.name: Grave Domain Spells
entries: has been updated to include more versatility and combat-relevant spells.

items.name: Path to the Grave
entries: now produces an ongoing curse effect in addition to boosting the damage of an attack.

items.name: Sentinel at Death's Door
entries: now can trigger on any attack, not just {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Critical Hits}.

items.name: Divine Reaper
entries: is a new feature that absorbs the previous benefits of {@subclassFeature Keeper of Souls|Cleric|PHB|Grave|XGE|17|XGE} and provides a boost to the Cleric's Necromancy spells.

entries.name: Design Note: Hexblade Patron Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Xanathar's Guide to Everything|XGE}:

items.name: Hexblade Spells
entries: (formerly {@subclassFeature The Hexblade|Warlock|PHB|Hexblade|XGE|1|XGE|Expanded Spell List}) now gives you a list of always prepared spells. The spells granted have been revised.

items.name: Hexblade Manifest
entries: is a new feature, allowing you to cast {@spell Hex|XPHB} a number of times without a spell slot. It also grants you a handful of unique ways to interact with your cursed target.

items.name: Life Stealer
entries: is a new feature that interacts with the {@spell Hex|XPHB} spell to provide healing and reliable damage.

items.name: Armor of Hexes
entries: now more reliably reduces damage taken.

items.name: Masterful Hex
entries: is a new feature that boosts the power of {@subclassFeature Hexblade Manifest|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}.

subclassFluff.name: Hollow Warden
entries: {@i Draw on the Might of Ancient Wild Terrors}

subclassFluff.name: Phantom
entries: {@i Embrace Death and Wield Ghostly Power}

entries.name: Design Note: Phantom Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Tasha's Cauldron of Everything|TCE}:

items.name: Wails from the Grave
entries: now links its number of uses to the Rogue's Dexterity modifier.

items.name: Tokens of the Departed
entries: now provides soul trinkets whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, allowing more reliable usage of the feature's benefits.

items.name: Voice of Death
entries: is a new feature that allows you to cast {@spell Speak with Dead|XPHB}.

items.name: Death's Friend
entries: now lets you gain a soul trinket when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no soul trinkets.

subclassFluff.name: Reanimator
entries: {@i Reassemble Corpses to Raise the Dead}

subclassFluff.name: Shadow Sorcery
entries: {@i Bend Darkness and Doom to Your Will}

entries.name: Design Note: Shadow Sorcery Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Xanathar's Guide to Everything|XGE}:

items.name: Eyes of the Dark
entries: now also grants you {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} and the ability to see through the {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} of any spell you cast.

items.name: Shadow Spells
entries: is a new feature that provides a list of always prepared spells.

items.name: Spirits of Ill Omen
entries: (formerly {@subclassFeature Hound of Ill Omen|Sorcerer|PHB|Shadow|XGE|6|XGE}) now uses the {@spell Summon Undead|XPHB} spell, allowing for more versatility and scaling.

items.name: Umbral Form
entries: now gives you one free use of the form per {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. {@subclassFeature Strength of the Grave|Sorcerer|PHB|Shadow|XGE|1|XGE} has been improved and moved to the benefits of this form.

subclassFluff.name: Undead Patron
entries: {@i Defy Death for Profane Power}

entries.name: Design Note: Undead Patron Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Van Richten's Guide to Ravenloft|VRGR}:

items.name: Undead Spells
entries: (formerly {@subclassFeature The Undead|Warlock|PHB|Undead|VRGR|1|VRGR|Expanded Spell List}) now gives you a list of always prepared spells.

items.name: Grave Touched
entries: now allows your spells and attacks to ignore {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

items.name: Necrotic Husk
entries: no longer requires a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to use its secondary feature, and it restores you to a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 10 times your Charisma modifier.

items.name: Superior Dread
entries: is a new feature that improves your {@subclassFeature Form of Dread|Warlock|XPHB|Undead|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}, allowing you to fly, regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and improve your spellcasting.

subclassFluff.name: Cavalier
entries: {@i Defend Your Allies on Foot or from a Mount}

entries.name: Design Note: Cavalier Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Xanathar's Guide to Everything|XGE}:

items.name: Unwavering Mark
entries: no longer has limited uses.

items.name: Ferocious Charger
entries: has been redesigned to give a burst of speed during the first round of each combat.

subclassFluff.name: Oathbreaker
entries: {@i Obtain Great Power at Any Cost}

entries.name: Design Note: Oathbreaker Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its last appearance in the 2014 {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG}:

items.name: Conjure Undead
entries: (formerly {@subclassFeature Control Undead|Paladin|PHB|Oathbreaker|DMG|3|DMG}) now lets you briefly summon Undead to aid you.

items.name: Dreadful Aspect
entries: now loops through the {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB} spell.

items.name: Oathbreaker Spells
entries: has new spells.

items.name: Aura of Hate
entries: now adds its effect to your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB}. The aura now benefits only Fiends and Undead that are your allies.

items.name: Dread Lord
entries: now imbues your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB} and can be recharged by expending a level 5 spell slot.

subclassFluff.name: Path of the Spiritual Guardian
entries: {@i Call on the Spirits for Combat Aid}

entries.name: Design Note: Path of the Spiritual Guardian Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass (formerly {@class Barbarian|PHB|Path of the Ancestral Guardian|Ancestral Guardian|XGE}) since its last appearance in {@book Xanathar's Guide to Everything|XGE}:

items.name: Spiritual Protectors
entries: (formerly {@subclassFeature Ancestral Protectors|Barbarian|PHB|Ancestral Guardian|XGE|3|XGE}) has been redesigned. The feature now splits the effects of the original feature, along with new effects, as options when you hit a creature while your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB} is active.

items.name: Spirit Shield
entries: now scales with your Rage Damage bonus.

items.name: Vengeful Spirits
entries: (formerly {@subclassFeature Vengeful Ancestors|Barbarian|PHB|Ancestral Guardian|XGE|14|XGE}) is a new feature that allows you to make an additional attack when you roll an 18 or higher on an attack roll.

subclassFluff.name: Path of the Storm Herald
entries: {@i Wield the Fury of the Storm}

entries.name: Design Note: Path of the Storm Herald Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@book Xanathar's Guide to Everything|XGE}:

items.name: Storm Aura
entries: now scales with your Rage Damage bonus. In addition, {@subclassFeature Tundra|Barbarian|XPHB|Storm Herald|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate|3|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate} has been redesigned to reduce a creature's damage instead of applying {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

items.name: Raging Storm
entries: has each environment redesigned. {@subclassFeature Desert|Barbarian|XPHB|Storm Herald|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate|14|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate} now makes a creature that failed the saving throw start burning. {@subclassFeature Sea|Barbarian|XPHB|Storm Herald|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate|14|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate} can now chain the lightning bolt to another creature. {@subclassFeature Tundra|Barbarian|XPHB|Storm Herald|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate|14|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate} now deals Cold damage and halves a creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of Intoxication
entries: {@i Drink Mystic Brews to Overwhelm and Confound Foes}

entries.name: Design Note: Warrior of Intoxication Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass (formerly {@class Monk|PHB|Way of the Drunken Master|Drunken Master|XGE}) since its last appearance in {@book Xanathar's Guide to Everything|XGE}:

items.name: Mystic Brew
entries: is a new feature that lets the {@class Monk|XPHB} create magical brews that they can drink to gain long-lasting benefits.

items.name: Master Brewer
entries: is a new feature that replaces {@subclassFeature Drunkard's Luck|Monk|PHB|Drunken Master|XGE|11|XGE}. The feature adds additional beverage options to {@subclassFeature Mystic Brew|Monk|XPHB|Intoxication|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate|6|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate}.

subclassFluff.name: Magic Stealer
entries: {@i Steal Magical Power to Empower Yourself}

subclassFluff.name: Oath of the Spellguard
entries: {@i Protect Your Allies from Villainous Magic}

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of the Mystic Arts
entries: {@i Weave Martial and Mystic Arts}

subclassFeature.name: Shroud of the Deceiver
entries: You have been initiated into the arts of the unseen. You gain the following benefits:

items.name: Gloom Sight:
items.entry: You gain Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet. You can see normally through areas of heavy obscurity caused by smoke, fog, or magical darkness created by your own spells or features.

items.name: Vicious Sneak Attack:
items.entry: Your strikes are lethal when unseen. When you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature while you are Invisible, in Darkness, or in an area of Heavy Obscurity, you roll d8s instead of d6s for your Sneak Attack damage.

subclassFeature.name: Cunning Strike: Choking Cloud
entries: You gain a new option for your Cunning Strike:

items.name: Choking Cloud (Cost: 1d6):
items.entry: You shatter a vial of caustic soot. A 10-foot-radius sphere of gray smoke appears centered on the target. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is Heavily Obscured. The smoke dissipates at the start of your next turn or if dispersed by a moderate or stronger wind. The target must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 \+ your Dexterity modifier \+ your Proficiency Bonus) or be Blinded until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Shadow Step
entries: At 9th level, you gain the ability to step from one shadow into another. When you are in dim light or darkness, you can use a Bonus Action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness. You have Advantage on the first melee attack you make before the end of the turn.

items.name: Shadow Sprint:
items.entry: If you use your Cunning Action to Dash or Disengage, you can weave this teleport into that movement, allowing you to teleport as part of that same Bonus Action.

subclassFeature.name: Lingering Hate
entries: The influence of your deity seeps into your blades, leaving a supernatural rot. When you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can curse it with lingering hate. The curse lasts for 1 minute or until the target dies.

items.name: Hexed Blade:
items.entry: While cursed, the target takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage whenever you hit it with an attack.

items.name: Cursed Mind/Body:
items.entry: When you apply the curse, choose one ability score; the target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with that ability.

entries: {@i Note: You can only have one creature cursed at a time, but this feature does not require concentration.}

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of Darkness
entries: You become a nightmare made manifest. You can cast {@i Greater Invisibility} on yourself without providing any components. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

items.name: Master of the Abyss:
items.entry: While you are Invisible or in an area of Heavy Obscurity, you can use two Cunning Strike options simultaneously on a single Sneak Attack, paying the Sneak Attack die cost for only the most expensive option.

subclassFeature.name: CQC Specialist
entries: Your training focuses on the brutal arts of the clinch and the takedown. You gain the following benefits:

items.name: Unarmed Prowess:
items.entry: Your unarmed strikes deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d8 \+ your Strength modifier.

items.name: Seamless Takedown:
items.entry: When you take the Attack action and hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can force them to make the saving throw against being Grappled or Shoved (your choice) as part of that same attack, rather than having to forfeit the damage.

items.name: Heavyweight Grappler:
items.entry: The saving throw DC to resist your Grapple or Shove attempts is calculated as {@b 8 \+ your Strength modifier \+ your Proficiency Bonus}. Furthermore, you can grapple creatures up to two sizes larger than you.

subclassFeature.name: The Strangler’s Hold
entries: When you have a creature Grappled, you can exert devastating control over their posture and breathing. You gain two maneuvers you can use against a creature currently grappled by you:

items.name: Chokehold:
items.entry: As a Bonus Action, you attempt to cut off the creature’s air or blood flow. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, the creature is Restrained until the grapple ends. While Restrained in this way, the creature is suffocating, cannot speak, and cannot cast spells requiring Verbal components.

items.name: Gale-Force Trip:
items.entry: When you hit a grappled creature with a melee attack, you can immediately force them to make a Strength saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, they are knocked Prone and take additional bludgeoning damage equal to your Fighter level. {@i (Note: Because a grappled creature has a Speed of 0, they cannot spend movement to stand up from Prone until they break the grapple).}

subclassFeature.name: Mean Streak
entries: You have learned to ruthlessly capitalize on an opponent's compromised posture.

items.name: Brutalized Advantage:
items.entry: Whenever you roll damage against a creature that is Prone, Grappled, or Restrained, you can roll the weapon or unarmed strike's damage dice twice and use the higher total.

items.name: Meat Shield:
items.entry: You know how to leverage your victim's body against incoming attacks. As long as you are grappling a creature of your size or larger, you gain Half-Cover (+2 to AC and Dexterity saving throws).

subclassFeature.name: Unstoppable Momentum
entries: Your physical conditioning reaches new heights, allowing you to dominate the arena without breaking a sweat.

items: Your Unarmed Prowess damage increases to 1d10.
Your movement speed is no longer halved when you are dragging or carrying a grappled creature.

subclassFeature.name: Pursuer
entries: You become an inescapable force on the battlefield.

items.name: Vaulting Leap:
items.entry: Your jump distance is doubled. If you move at least 10 feet before leaping toward a creature, you can attempt to Grapple them as a Bonus Action upon landing.

items.name: Submission Master:
items.entry: Your Chokehold becomes lethal. When a creature starts its turn Restrained by your Chokehold, they must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, they gain one level of Exhaustion. This exhaustion is removed when they finish a Short or Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Apex Predator
entries: You have reached the pinnacle of physical dominance. You gain the following:

items.name: Colossal Grip:
items.entry: You can now grapple creatures of any size.

items.name: Neck Break:
items.entry: When you have a creature Restrained via your Chokehold, you can use your Action to attempt a horrific finishing move. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, they take 10d10 Force damage, or half as much on a success. If this damage reduces the creature to 0 hit points, you snap their neck or spine, killing them instantly (provided the creature has an anatomy that allows for this).

subclassFeature.name: Fate Domain Spells
entries: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Fate Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

rows: {@spell Augury}, {@spell Bless}, {@spell Fortune's Favor}, {@spell Silvery Barbs}

subclassFeature.name: Prophet of Truth
entries: You can nudge the scales of destiny in moments of crisis. When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet makes a D20 Test (an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw), you can use your Reaction to roll a 1d6 and add or subtract it from the total. You can choose to use this feature after the d20 is rolled, but before the DM declares whether the roll succeeds or fails.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Strands of Fate
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to tether the fates of those around you. As a Magic Action, you choose a number of willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, up to your Wisdom modifier. For 1 minute, you create a glowing, ethereal "Fate Bond" between them and yourself. While bonded, you unlock the following capabilities:

items.name: Tethered Vitality:
items.entry: Whenever a bonded creature takes damage, you can use your Reaction to halve that damage. One other bonded creature of your choice (which can be you) then takes an amount of Force damage equal to the prevented damage. This transferred damage cannot be reduced or avoided in any way.

items.name: Shared Destiny:
items.entry: Once per round, when a bonded creature makes a D20 Test, they can choose to replace their roll with the natural d20 result most recently rolled by another bonded creature. They must choose to do this before the outcome of their roll is determined.

subclassFeature.name: Ties that Bind
entries: Your mastery over the timeline deepens, allowing you to sever harmful threads of fate. When you use your {@i Strands of Fate} Channel Divinity, you can immediately end one spell or condition affecting one of the bonded creatures. The condition must be Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Destiny
entries: You have seen the end of all things, and nothing catches you off guard. You can no longer be Surprised, and you have Advantage on all Initiative rolls.

items.name: Visions of the Future:
items.entry: You can cast the {@i Foresight} spell once per Long Rest without expending a spell slot or requiring material components. When you cast it using this feature, its duration is reduced to 10 minutes, but you can choose one allied creature within 30 feet to also gain the full benefits of the spell for the duration.

subclassFeature.name: Vestige Companion
entries: The vestige manifests to aid you, drawing strength from your pact. It uses the {@b Vestige Companion} stat block and is a Celestial, Fiend, or Undead (choose when you gain this feature). You determine its appearance, but regardless of its form, it bears features indicating its supernatural origin. The vestige is Friendly to you and your allies and obeys your commands. It vanishes if you die.

{@b The Vestige in Combat.} In combat, the vestige acts during your turn. It can move and use its Reaction on its own, but the only action it takes is the Dodge action unless you take a Bonus Action to command it to take an action in its stat block or some other action. You can also sacrifice one of your attacks when you take the Attack action to command the vestige to take the {@i Vestige's Strike} action. If you have the Incapacitated condition, the vestige acts on its own and isn't limited to the Dodge action.
{@b Divine Disposition.} The vestige's power is unstable, and its willingness to aid you depends on its current disposition. At the start of combat, its disposition is {@b Neutral}.

items.name: Neutral:
items.entry: The vestige follows your commands normally as outlined above.

items.name: Displeased:
items.entry: If the vestige takes damage, its disposition becomes Displeased. While Displeased, its {@i Vestige's Strike} attack rolls are made with Disadvantage.

items.name: Favorable:
items.entry: As a Bonus Action, you or an ally within 5 feet of the vestige can offer a prayer or tend to its vulnerable form by making a DC 10 Charisma (Persuasion or Religion) check. On a success, its disposition improves by one step (Displeased to Neutral, or Neutral to Favorable). While Favorable, its {@i Vestige's Strike} attack rolls are made with Advantage.

entries: {@b Disappearance of the Vestige.} When the vestige drops to 0 Hit Points, it disappears. You can spend 1 minute performing a magic ceremony to manifest the vestige again; it reappears with its maximum Hit Points in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. As a Magic action, you can temporarily dismiss the vestige to a pocket dimension, and as a Magic action while it is dismissed, you can cause it to reappear in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you.
Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can summon the vestige in a new form, choosing its appearance and whether it is a Celestial, Fiend, or Undead.

subclassFeature.name: Vestige Spells
entries: The magic of your Vestige Companion ensures you always have certain spells ready. Select one of the following Cleric Domains: Life, Light, Trickery, or War. The Domain Spells of your chosen Domain are Warlock spells for you. When you reach a Warlock level equal to a Cleric Level listed on the Domain Spells table for the Domain you have chosen, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Power
entries: Once per day as a Bonus Action, your vestige manifests a remnant of its divine power in one of the following ways, based on its form. {@i The vestige cannot use this feature while its disposition is Displeased.}

items.name: Cursed Invocation (Undead Only).
items.entry: The vestige places a curse on a creature you can see within 30 feet of the vestige for 1 minute. While cursed, the target has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you and the vestige.

items.name: Fiendish Swap (Fiend Only).
items.entry: If you and the vestige are within 60 feet of each other, you both teleport, swapping places.

items.name: Healing Touch (Celestial Only).
items.entry: The vestige touches another creature. The target regains Hit Points equal to 2d8 plus your Charisma modifier and ends one condition on it: Blinded, Deafened, or Poisoned.

subclassFeature.name: Vestige Recovery
entries: Your Vestige Companion now regains its use of Divine Power whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest or when you use your Magical Cunning feature. Additionally, if you spend time tending to the vestige during a Short Rest, its disposition automatically becomes {@b Favorable} at the start of your next combat encounter.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Power
entries: Your Vestige Companion increases in magical strength. As a Magic Action, you can cause the vestige to manifest an aura in a 30-foot Emanation that originates from the vestige. The aura lasts a number of hours equal to your Charisma modifier or until the vestige disappears or is temporarily dismissed. You, the vestige, and your allies within the aura gain Resistance to Fire, Necrotic, and Radiant damage and have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions. Once you manifest this aura, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

In addition, when you drop to 0 Hit Points while within the aura, you instead change your Hit Points to a number equal to your Warlock level plus your Charisma modifier. The vestige is then dismissed into a pocket dimension and can't return until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Semblance of Life
entries: Your Vestige Companion continues to grow in strength, and with your assistance, it can briefly adopt a more powerful form. Depending on the vestige's type, you can cast one of the following spells while the vestige is within 90 feet of you without expending a spell slot or needing Material components: {@i Summon Celestial} (Celestial vestige), {@i Summon Fiend} (Fiend vestige), or {@i Summon Undead} (Undead vestige).

When you cast the spell with this feature, the vestige becomes the summoned creature. Its game statistics are replaced by the summoned creature's stat block, the spell's level is equal to half your Warlock level (round down; maximum level 9), and the spell's duration is 1 minute. When the spell ends, the vestige returns to its previous form. Once you have cast the spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Awareness
entries: Your connection to wild magic allows you to sense its presence. As a Magic Action, you can cast the {@i Detect Magic} spell without expending a spell slot or requiring components. Strength is your spellcasting ability for this spell. When you cast it using this feature, its range is 60 feet, and it does not require Concentration.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Strength modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Surge
entries: The magical energy roiling inside you sometimes erupts from you. When you enter your Rage, you roll on your Wild Magic d100 table to determine the magical effect produced.
If a Wild Magic effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals {@b 8 \+ your Strength modifier \+ your Proficiency Bonus}.

subclassFeature.name: Bolstering Magic
entries: You can harness your wild magic to bolster yourself or a companion. As a Magic Action, you can touch a willing creature (which can be yourself) and confer one of the following benefits of your choice:

items.name: Might:
items.entry: For 10 minutes, whenever the creature makes a D20 Test (an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw), it can roll a d4 and add it to the result.

items.name: Mana:
items.entry: The creature rolls a d4 and regains an expended spell slot of a level equal to the number rolled or lower (the creature’s choice). Once a creature receives this benefit, it can’t receive it again until it finishes a Long Rest.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Strength modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Precision
entries: {@i (Moved from Level 5 to fit standard Barbarian progression)}

The chaotic energy surrounding you parts the magical defenses of your foes. While your Rage is active, your melee weapon attacks ignore spells and magical effects that reduce, nullify, or reflect damage (such as {@i Armor of Agathys}, {@i Fire Shield}, or {@i Blink}). Standard damage Resistances and Immunities still apply normally.

subclassFeature.name: Unstable Backlash
entries: When you are imperiled, the magic within you lashes out defensively. When you take damage or fail a saving throw while your Rage is active, you can use your Reaction to roll on the Wild Magic d100 table and immediately produce the effect. This new effect replaces your current active Wild Magic effect.

subclassFeature.name: Controlled Surge
entries: You have learned to ride the chaotic currents of your magic. Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic d100 table, you can roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to unleash. If you roll the exact same number on both dice, you can ignore the roll and choose any effect on the table.

subclassFeature.name: Thunderous Blows
entries: Starting when you choose this path, your rage instills you with the strength to batter around your foes, making any battlefield your domain. Once per turn while your Rage is active, when you damage a creature with a melee attack, you can force the target to make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 \+ your Strength modifier \+ your Proficiency Bonus).
On a failed save, you push the target 5 feet away from you, and you can choose to immediately move up to 5 feet into the target’s previous space without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Stance of the Mountain
entries: You harness your fury to anchor your feet to the earth, shrugging off the blows of those who wish to topple you. While your Rage is active, you cannot be knocked Prone unless you have the Unconscious condition.

subclassFeature.name: Demolishing Might
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you muster destructive force with your assault, shaking the core of even the strongest structures. You gain the following benefits:

items.name: Siege Engine:
items.entry: Your melee attacks deal double damage to objects and structures. Furthermore, your melee attacks against creatures of the Construct type deal an additional 1d8 damage of the weapon's type.

items.name: Diligent Discipline:
items.entry: When you successfully push a creature or knock it Prone (such as with an Unarmed Strike or a Weapon Mastery like Push or Topple), the next time you hit that creature with an improvised weapon or melee attack before the end of your turn, the attack deals an extra 1d8 Thunder damage, and the creature is pushed up to 15 feet away from you. {@i (This Thunder damage increases to 2d8 when you reach Barbarian level 10).}

subclassFeature.name: Overwhelming Cleave
entries: Upon reaching 10th level, you wade into armies of foes, great swings of your weapon striking any who threaten you. While your Rage is active, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use a Bonus Action to make one additional melee weapon attack against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach.

subclassFeature.name: Unstoppable
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can choose to become an unstoppable force when you activate your Rage. If you do so, for the duration of the Rage your Speed cannot be reduced, and you are immune to the Frightened, Paralyzed, and Stunned conditions.

If you are currently afflicted by the Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned conditions, you can still take a Bonus Action to activate your Rage and suspend those effects for the duration of your Rage. When an Unstoppable Rage ends, the sheer physical toll causes you to gain one level of Exhaustion.

subclassFeature.name: Seeker Spells
entries: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Warlock level specified in the Seeker Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
{@b Seeker Spells Table}

rows: {@spell Feather Fall}, {@spell Jump}, {@spell Levitate}, {@spell Locate Object}

subclassFeature.name: Shielding Aurora
entries: You can invoke your patron's power to protect you from harm as you traverse dangerous realms. As a Bonus Action, you create a whirling aurora of brilliant cosmic energy that swirls around you.

Until the end of your next turn, you gain Resistance to all damage, and if a hostile creature ends its turn within 10 feet of you, it takes Radiant damage equal to your Warlock level \+ your Charisma modifier.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Chain of the Cosmos
entries: Your patron grants you a physical manifestation of your bond, often appearing as a chain forged from starlight, an ancient astrolabe, or a glowing pendant.

While this item is on your person, you always have the {@i Augury} spell prepared, and you can cast it as a Ritual without requiring Material components. Furthermore, you can invoke the item's power to gain Advantage on an Intelligence check. Once you use this Advantage feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
If you lose this item, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony during a Short or Long Rest to receive a replacement, which destroys the previous item.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Refuge
entries: You gain the ability to step into an astral refuge, dodging danger and rapidly preparing your magical defenses. As a Magic Action, you disappear from the world for a brief moment and enter the Astral Plane, taking advantage of its timeless nature.

While in your astral refuge, you can prepare to use two Actions to cast spells. After you prepare these two actions, you instantly return to the space you occupied (or the nearest unoccupied space), the spells are cast and your turn ends.
Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest, unless you expend a Warlock spell slot (no action required) to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Far Wanderer
entries: Your body adapts to the harsh, unforgiving environments of the cosmos. You no longer need to breathe, and you gain Resistance to Fire damage and Cold damage.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Sequestration
entries: You gain the ability to sequester yourself and your allies within a localized pocket of the Astral Plane. By performing a special ritual over the course of 1 minute, you shift yourself and up to ten willing creatures you can see into the Astral Plane.

You and those creatures gain the benefits of a Short Rest while sequestered on the Astral Plane. You then return to the spaces you all occupied when you used this ability, with no time having passed in the material world. During this special Short Rest, you and the sequestered creatures can make use of any options normally available during a rest.
Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sea Magic
entries: Your connection to the ocean grants you access to certain spells. When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Sea Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. Each of these spells counts as a Sorcerer spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of Sorcerer spells you can prepare.

rows: {@spell Fog Cloud}, {@spell Thunderwave}, {@spell Misty Step}, {@spell Elminster’s Hand of Water}

subclassFeature.name: Soul of the Sea
entries: Your body is imbued with the physical resilience of the deep. You can breathe air and water, and you gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

subclassFeature.name: Aqueous Shield
entries: You can call upon the ocean to protect you. As a Bonus Action, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to envelop yourself in a swirling shield of magical water for 1 minute. While the shield is active, you gain a \+2 bonus to your Armor Class.

When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, the shield violently erupts and the effect ends. The attacker takes Cold damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Proficiency Bonus, and the target becomes {@b Drenched} until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Drenched:
