{@b {@i Triboelectric.}} When you choose your Arcane Tempest option, any creature that failed its save also takes 2d6 Lightning or Thunder damage (your choice). If your Innate Sorcery is active, the damage is 3d6 instead.

subclassFeature.name: Wind Soul
entries: The storm inside of you becomes more powerful.
{@b {@i One with the Storm.}} You have immunity to Lightning and Thunder damage and you have a Fly Speed of 60 feet.

{@b {@i Blessing of the Tempest.}} As a Magic action, you can reduce your Fly Speed to 30 feet for 1 hour and choose a number of creatures within 30 feet of you equal to 3 \+ your Charisma modifier. The chosen creatures gain a Fly Speed of 30 feet for 1 hour. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Spell list added}
{@i Moved level 1 features to level 3}
{@i New level 3 feature Storm Within that incorporates the old level 1 feature. Allows you to choose between two options when casting a Sorc spell. Innate Sorcery boosts these features.}
{@i New level 6 feature Stormladen, that besides granting resistance also lets you freely change the damage of a spell you cast to lightning or thunder.}
{@i Second new level 6 feature Arcane Conductivity lets you arc your magic to a couple of extra targets. Innate Sorcery boosts the potency of it}
{@i New level 14 feature Heart of the Storm boosts the Storm Within feature with sorcery points}

subclassFeature.name: Patron Spells
entries: The magic of your otherworldly patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Fathomless Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
##### Fathomless Spells

rows: {@spell Create or Destroy Water}, {@spell Gust of Wind}, {@spell Thunderwave}, {@spell Silence}
{@spell Bigby’s Hand (appears as a tentacle)}, {@spell Conjure Elemental}

subclassFeature.name: Tentacle of the Deeps
entries: You can magically summon a spectral tentacle that strikes at your foes. As a Bonus Action, you create a 10-foot-long tentacle at a point you can see within 60 feet of you. The tentacle lasts for 1 minute or until you use this feature to create another tentacle.

When you create the tentacle, you can make a melee spell attack against one creature within 10 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Cold damage, and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. When you reach level 10 in this class, the damage increases to 2d8.

As a Bonus Action on your turn, you can move the tentacle up to 30 feet and repeat the attack. You can summon the tentacle a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Gift of the Sea
entries: You gain a Swim Speed of 40 feet, and you can breathe underwater.

subclassFeature.name: Oceanic Soul
entries: You are now even more at home in the depths. You have Resistance to Cold damage. In addition, when you are fully submerged, any creature that is also fully submerged can understand your speech, and you can understand theirs.

subclassFeature.name: Guardian Coil
entries: Your Tentacle of the Deeps can defend you and others, interposing itself between them and harm. When you or a creature you can see takes damage while within 10 feet of the tentacle, you can take a Reaction to choose one of those creatures and reduce the damage to that creature by an amount equal to 1d8 plus half your Warlock level.

subclassFeature.name: Grasping Tentacles
entries: You can cast the {@i Evard’s Black Tentacles} spell without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

When you cast this spell, your patron’s magic ensures the spell’s resiliency. Your Concentration on the spell is only broken if you have the Incapacitated condition, and Concentrating on it does not prevent you from Concentrating on other Warlock spells.

subclassFeature.name: Fathomless Tentacles
entries: As a Magic action, enormous spectral tentacles burst from your surroundings, which grab you and up to 5 creatures of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. The tentacles move each creature to an unoccupied space you can see of your choice within 120 feet of its starting position, and then disappear. Unwilling creatures can make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC to avoid being moved.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Level 1 features moved to level 3\.}
{@i Spell list has been made always prepared}
{@i Summon Elemental and Cone of Cold have been replaced with Evard’s Black Tentacles and Conjure Elemental.}
{@i Tentacle of the Deeps can be used a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier, instead of proficiency bonus}
{@i Guardian Coil’s damage reduction has been changed from 1d8 (later 2d8) to be 1d8 plus half your Warlock level.}
{@i Grasping Tentacles no longer grants temporary hit points when you cast Black Tentacles, but Black Tentacles now doesn’t prevent you from Concentrating on other Warlock spells}
{@i The capstone, which was previously a very situational teleport, has been redesigned into a battlefield repositioning ability.}

subclassFeature.name: Patron Spells
entries: The magic of your otherworldly patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Genie Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. You prepare the spells in the Genie Spells column and the column appropriate to your patron’s Genie Kind. In addition, the {@i Wish} spell is added to the Warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Genie’s Vessel
entries: Your patron gifts you a magical vessel that grants you a measure of the genie’s power. The vessel is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells. You decide what the object is.

rows: {@spell Detect Evil and Good}, {@spell Phantasmal Force}

entries: {@b Bottled Respite.} As a Magic action, you can touch the vessel and magically vanish and enter your vessel, which remains in the space you left. The interior of the vessel is an extradimensional space in the shape of a 20-foot-radius Cylinder, which is 20 feet high, and resembles your vessel. The interior is appointed with cushions and low tables and is a comfortable temperature. While inside, you can hear the area around your vessel as if you were in its space. You can remain inside the vessel up to a number of hours equal to twice your Charisma modifier (minimum, 1 hour). You exit the vessel early if you use a Bonus Action to leave, if you die, or if the vessel is destroyed. When you exit the vessel, you appear in the unoccupied space closest to it. Any objects left in the vessel remain there until carried out, and if the vessel is destroyed, every object stored there harmlessly appears in the unoccupied spaces closest to the vessel’s former space. Once you enter the vessel, you can’t enter again until you finish a Long Rest.

{@b {@i Genie’s Wrath.}} Once during each of your turns, when you hit with an attack roll while touching the vessel, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1 damage). The type of this damage is determined by your patron’s Genie Kind.
{@b {@i Vessel Statistics.}} The vessel’s AC equals your spell save DC. Its Hit Points equal your Warlock level plus your proficiency bonus, and it has Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage.

{@b {@i Restoring the Vessel.}} If the vessel is destroyed or you lose it, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement from your patron. This ceremony can be performed during a Short or Long Rest, and the previous vessel is destroyed if it still exists. The vessel vanishes in a flare of elemental power when you die.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Gift
entries: You begin to take on characteristics of your patron’s kind. You now have resistance to a damage type determined by your patron’s Genie Kind.

In addition, as a Bonus Action, you can give yourself a Fly Speed of 30 feet that lasts for 10 minutes, during which you can hover. You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sanctuary Vessel
entries: When you enter your Genie’s Vessel via the Bottled Respite feature, you can now choose up to five willing creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you, and the chosen creatures are drawn into the vessel with you.

As a Bonus Action, you can eject any number of creatures from the vessel, and everyone is ejected if you leave or die or if the vessel is destroyed. In addition, anyone (including you) who remains within the vessel for at least 10 minutes gains the benefit of finishing a Short Rest, and anyone can add your Charisma modifier (minimum 1\) to the number of Hit Points they regain if they spend any Hit Dice as part of a Short Rest there.

subclassFeature.name: Limited Wish
entries: You entreat your patron to grant you a small wish. As a Magic action, you can speak your desire to your Genie’s Vessel, requesting the effect of one spell that is level 6 or lower and has a casting time of an action. The spell can be from any class’s spell list, and you don’t need to meet the requirements in that spell, including costly components; the spell simply takes effect as part of this action.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Level 1 features moved to level 3\.}
{@i Spell list has been made always prepared, except for Wish, which is made an option for level 9 mystic arcanum}
{@i Phantasmal Killer is replaced with Summon Elemental}
{@i Elemental Gift’s flying speed is now tied to Charisma modifier instead of Proficiency Bonus}
{@i The extra hit points regained in Sanctuary Vessel are now tied to Charisma Modifier instead of Proficiency Bonus}
{@i Limited Wish can now be used once per Long Rest, rather than once ever 1d4 Long Rests.}

subclassFeature.name: Wizardly Quill
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can magically create a Tiny quill in your free hand. The magic quill has the following properties:
{@b {@i Inkless.}} The quill doesn’t require ink. When you write with it, it produces non-magical ink in a color of your choice on the writing surface.
{@b {@i Quick Copying.}} The time you must spend to copy a spell into your spellbook equals 2 minutes per spell level if you use the quill for the transcription. The cost is also reduced by half.
{@b {@i Arcane Eraser.}} You can erase anything you write with the quill if you wave the feather over the text as a Bonus Action, provided the text is within 5 feet of you.
This quill disappears if you create another one or if you die.

subclassFeature.name: Awakened Spellbook
entries: Using specially prepared inks and ancient incantations passed down by your wizardly tradition, you have awakened an arcane sentience within your spellbook. While you are holding the book, it grants you the following benefits:
{@b {@i Spellcasting Focus.}} You can use the book as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.

{@b {@i Damage Swap.}} When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot, you can temporarily replace its damage type with a type that appears in another spell in your spellbook, which magically alters the spell’s formula for this casting only. The latter spell must be of the same level as the spell slot you expend.

{@b {@i Quick Ritual.}} When you cast a Wizard spell as a ritual, you can use the spell’s normal casting time, rather than adding 10 minutes to it. Once you use this benefit, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

If necessary, you can replace the book over the course of a Short Rest by using your Wizardly Quill to write arcane sigils in a blank book or a magic spellbook to which you’re attuned. At the end of the rest, your spellbook’s consciousness is summoned into the new book, which the consciousness transforms into your spellbook, along with all its spells. If the previous book still existed somewhere, all the spells vanish from its pages.

subclassFeature.name: Manifest Mind
entries: You can conjure forth the mind of your Awakened Spellbook.

{@b {@i Conjuring the Mind.}} As a Bonus Action while the book is on your person, you can cause the mind to manifest as a Tiny spectral object, hovering in an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet of you. The spectral mind is intangible and doesn’t occupy its space, and it sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius. It looks like a ghostly tome, a cascade of text, or a scholar from the past (your choice).

{@b {@i Senses.}} While manifested, the spectral mind can hear and see, and it has darkvision with a range of 60 feet. The mind can telepathically share with you what it sees and hears (no action required).

{@b {@i Shared Spellcasting.}} Whenever you cast a Wizard spell on your turn, you can cast it as if you were in the spectral mind’s space, instead of your own, using its senses. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
{@b {@i Moving the Mind.}} As a Bonus Action, you can cause the spectral mind to Move up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you or it can see. It can pass through creatures but not objects.

{@b {@i Duration.}} The spectral mind stops manifesting if it is ever more than 300 feet away from you, if someone casts {@i Dispel Magic} on it, if the Awakened Spellbook is destroyed, if you die, or if you dismiss the spectral mind as a Bonus Action. Once you conjure the mind, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest, unless you expend a spell slot of level 1 or higher to conjure it again.

subclassFeature.name: Master Scrivener
entries: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can create one magic scroll by touching your Wizardly Quill to a blank piece of paper or parchment and causing one spell from your Awakened Spellbook to be copied onto the scroll.

The spellbook must be within 5 feet of you when you make the scroll. The chosen spell must be of level 1 or 2 and must have a casting time of an action. Once in the scroll, the spell’s power is enhanced, counting as one level higher than normal. You can cast the spell from the scroll by reading it as a Magic action. The scroll is unintelligible to anyone else, and the spell vanishes from the scroll when you cast it or when you finish your next Long Rest.

You are also adept at crafting spell scrolls, which are described in Chapter 6 of the Player’s Handbook. The gold and time you must spend to make such a scroll are halved if you use your Wizardly Quill.

subclassFeature.name: One with the Word
entries: Your connection to your Awakened Spellbook has become so profound that your soul has become entwined with it. While the book is on your person, you have Advantage on all Intelligence (Arcana) checks, as the spellbook helps you remember magical lore.

Moreover, if you take damage while your spellbook’s mind is manifested, you can prevent all of that damage to you by using your Reaction to dismiss the spectral mind, using its magic to save yourself. Then roll 3d6. The spellbook temporarily loses spells of your choice that have a combined spell level equal to that roll or higher. For example, if the roll’s total is 9, spells vanish from the book that have a combined level of at least 9, which could mean one 9th-level spell, three level 3 spells, or some other combination. If there aren’t enough spells in the book to cover this cost, you drop to 0 Hit Points.

Until you finish 1d3 Long Rests, you are incapable of casting the lost spells, even if you find them on a scroll or in another spellbook. After you finish the required number of rests, the spells reappear in the spellbook. Once you use this Reaction, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Level 2 features moved to level 3\.}
{@i The cost of copying a spell into your spellbook is cut in half by using the Wizardly Quill.}
{@i You must now see the space the manifested mind is conjured to.}
{@i The number of times you can cast a spell from the Manifested Mind’s space is now your Intelligence Modifier}
{@i One with the Word now removes spells from your spellbook for 1d3 Long Rests, instead of 1d6.}

subclassFeature.name: War and Song
entries: You have Armor Training with Light Armor and you have proficiency in one martial melee weapon that lacks the Two-Handed property and in the Performance skill. If you already have proficiency in the Performance skill, you gain proficiency in another skill available to Wizards at level 1\.

subclassFeature.name: Bladesong
entries: Using your Bonus Action, you can invoke an elven magic called the Bladesong, provided that you aren't wearing medium or heavy armor or using a shield. It graces you with supernatural speed, agility, and focus for 1 minute. It ends early if you have the Incapacitated condition, if you don Medium Armor, Heavy Armor or a Shield, if you use two hands to make an attack with a weapon, or if you die. You can also dismiss the Bladesong at any time (no action required). While your Bladesong is active, you gain the following benefits:
{@b {@i Keen Defense.}} You gain a bonus to your Armor Class equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of \+1).
{@b {@i Increased Speed.}} Your Speed increases by 10 feet.
{@b {@i Deft Movement.}} You have Advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks.
{@b {@i Hyperfocus.}} You gain a bonus to any Constitution saving throw you make to maintain your Concentration on a spell. The bonus equals your Intelligence modifier (minimum of \+1).
You can use your Bladesong a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
In addition, you can cast one of your cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Song of Defense
entries: You can direct your magic to absorb damage while your Bladesong is active. When you take damage, you can use your Reaction to expend one spell slot and reduce the damage to take by an amount equal to five times the spell slot's level.

subclassFeature.name: Song of Victory
entries: While your Bladesong is active, you can add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of \+1) to the damage of your melee attacks.
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Level 2 features moved to level 3\.}
{@i Added a replacement proficiency in case you already have proficiency in Performance at level 3\.}
{@i Added a clause that Bladesong ends early if you die}
{@i Bladesong has a number of uses equal to your Intelligence modifier.}
{@i Extra Attack now restricts you to cantrips that have an action casting time, matching the Eldritch Knight’s update.}
{@i Song of Victory now adds damage to all of your Melee attacks.}

subclassFeature.name: Chronal Shift
entries: You can magically exert limited control over the flow of time around a creature. As a Reaction, after you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes a D20 Test, you can force the creature to reroll. The target must use the result of the second roll. You can use this ability twice, and you regain any expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Temporal Awareness
entries: Your ability to peer into the different possible futures gives you an edge in combat. You have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Momentary Stasis
entries: As a Magic action, you can force a Large or smaller creature you can see within 60 feet of you to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is encased in a field of magical energy until the end of your next turn or until the creature takes any damage. While encased in this way, the creature has the Incapacitated condition and has a Speed of 0\. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of 1). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Abeyance
entries: When you cast a spell with a casting time of an action using a spell slot of level 4 or lower, you can condense the spell's magic into a mote. The spell is frozen in time at the moment of casting and held within a gray bead for 1 hour. This bead is a Tiny object with AC 15 and 1 hit point, and it is immune to Poison and Psychic damage. When the duration ends, or if the bead is destroyed, it vanishes in a flash of light, and the spell is lost.

A creature holding the bead can take the Magic action to release the spell within, whereupon the bead disappears. The spell uses your spell attack bonus and save DC, and the spell treats the creature who released it as the caster for all other purposes.
Once you create a bead with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Convergent Future
entries: You can peer through possible futures and magically pull one of them into events around you, ensuring a particular outcome. When you or a creature you can see within 60 feet of you makes a D20 Test, you can use your Reaction to ignore the die roll and decide whether the number rolled is the minimum needed to succeed or one less than that number (your choice).
When you use this feature, you gain one level of Exhaustion. Only by finishing a Long Rest can you remove a level of exhaustion gained in this way.
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Level 2 features moved to level 3\.}
{@i Arcane Abeyance can only be used on spells that have a casting time of an action}

subclassFeature.name: Adjust Density
entries: As a Magic action, you can alter the weight of one object or creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The object or creature must be Large or smaller. The target's weight is halved or doubled for up to 1 minute or until your Concentration ends (as if you were Concentrating on a spell). Upon reaching level 10 in this class, you can target an object or a creature that is Huge or smaller.
{@b {@i Halved Weight.}} The creature's Speed increases by 10 feet, it can jump twice as far as normal, and it has Disadvantage on Strength checks and Strength saves.
{@b {@i Doubled Weight.}} The creature's Speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it has Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saves.

subclassFeature.name: Gravity Well
entries: Whenever you cast a spell on a creature, you can move the target 5 feet to an unoccupied space of your choice if the target is willing to move, the spell hits it with an attack, or it fails a saving throw against the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Violent Attraction
entries: You can manipulate the velocity of creatures and objects.

{@b {@i Increase Velocity.}} When another creature that you can see within 60 feet of you hits with an attack, you can use your Reaction to increase the attack's velocity, causing the attack's target to take an extra 1d10 damage of the weapon's type.
{@b {@i Accelerate Fall.}} If a creature within 60 feet of you takes damage from a fall, you can use your reaction to increase the fall's damage by 2d10.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Event Horizon
entries: As a Magic action, you can emit a powerful field of gravitational energy that tugs at other creatures for up to 1 minute or until your Concentration ends (as if you were Concentrating on a spell). For the duration, whenever a creature hostile to you starts its turn within 30 feet of you, it must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it takes 2d10 Force damage, and its Speed is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage, and every foot it moves this turn costs 2 extra feet of movement.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest or until you expend a spell slot of level 3 or higher to use it again.
{@i Update Log:}

items: {@i Language changes}
{@i Level 2 features moved to level 3\.}

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Deflection
entries: You have learned to weave your magic to fortify yourself against harm. When you are hit by an attack or you fail a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to gain a \+2 bonus to your AC or a \+4 bonus to saving throws using that ability score, potentially turning the hit into a miss or a failure into a success. The bonus lasts until the start of your next turn.
When you use this feature, you can’t cast spells of a level higher than one fifth your Wizard level (round down, minimum level 0\) until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Wit
entries: Your keen ability to assess tactical situations allows you to act quickly in battle. You have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Power Surge
entries: You can store magical energy within yourself to later empower your damaging spells. In its stored form, this energy is called a Power Surge.
{@b {@i Free Preparations.}} You add the {@i Counterspell} and {@i Dispel Magic} spells to your spellbook for free, if they weren’t there already, and you always have them prepared.

{@b {@i Number of Power Surges.}} You can store a maximum number of Power Surges equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one). Whenever you finish a Long Rest, your number of Power Surges resets to one. If you end a Short Rest with no power surges, you gain one Power surge.

{@b {@i Gaining Power Surges.}} Whenever you successfully end a spell with Dispel Magic or Counterspell, you gain one Power Surge, as you steal magic from the spell you foiled. You also gain one Power Surge when you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with a Wizard spell that used a spell slot, but only once per casting.

{@b {@i Spell Empowerment.}} Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature or object with a Wizard spell, you can spend one Power Surge to deal extra Force damage to that target. The extra damage equals your Wizard level.

subclassFeature.name: Durable Magic
entries: The magic you channel helps ward off harm. While you maintain Concentration on a spell, you have a \+2 bonus to AC and all saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Gladiator
entries: Gladiators are just as much performers as they are warriors. Whether brawling in underground fighting rings or fighting for survival in bloodstained arenas, a Gladiator combines martial skill with theatrics to awe and intimidate their audiences.

subclassFeature.name: Brutality
entries: Shaped by the intensity of the arena, you've learned to execute brutal martial moves. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a Melee weapon, you can add one of the following Brutality effects of your choice. You can do this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Combat Theatrics
entries: You've honed your combat skills to entertain, devastating opponents and dazzling onlookers. You gain the following benefits.

items.name: Athletic Flair
entries: Whenever you make a Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) or Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1).

items.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: You gain {@variantrule proficiency|XPHB} in one of these skills of your choice: {@skill Acrobatics}, {@skill Athletics}, {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, or {@skill Performance}.

subclassFeature.name: Bleed
entries: You can activate the {@itemMastery Sap|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon, and the target takes extra damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 damage). The extra damage's type is the same type as the weapon's type

subclassFeature.name: Bluff
entries: You can activate the {@itemMastery Vex|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw you make before the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Stumble
entries: You can activate the {@itemMastery Topple|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon, and on its next turn, the target can take only an {@variantrule action|XPHB} or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, not both.

subclassFeature.name: Flourish Parry
entries: You've learned to stylishly counter blows from your opponents. When an enemy hits you with a melee attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1) to your AC against the attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.

entries.name: Flourish Counter
entries: If this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} causes the attack to miss, you can retaliate with a powerful counterattack as part of the same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. Make an attack roll with a Melee weapon against the triggering creature. If this attack hits, you can use one of your {@subclassFeature Brutality|Fighter|XPHB|Gladiator|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses|3|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses} effects on the target without expending a use of that feature.

Once this counterattack hits, you can't use this feature to make another counterattack until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of this counterattack by expending a use of {@classFeature Second Wind|Fighter|XPHB|1|XPHB} (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Bolder Brutalities
entries: Your brutal martial prowess has improved. The following effects are added to your {@subclassFeature Brutality|Fighter|XPHB|Gladiator|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses|3|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses} options.

subclassFeature.name: Rive
entries: You can activate the {@itemMastery Cleave|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon, and you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the additional attack made as part of that property's activation.

subclassFeature.name: Rush
entries: You can activate the {@itemMastery Push|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon, and you can immediately move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

subclassFeature.name: Stagger
entries: You can activate the {@itemMastery Slow|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon, and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Brutal Resurgence
entries: Whenever you use your {@classFeature Second Wind|Fighter|XPHB|1|XPHB} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain an expended use of {@subclassFeature Brutality|Fighter|XPHB|Gladiator|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses|3|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses}. You also regain an expended use of {@subclassFeature Brutality|Fighter|XPHB|Gladiator|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses|3|XUA2025ApocalypticSubclasses} whenever you use your {@classFeature Action Surge|Fighter|XPHB|2|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Mutilate
entries: When you hit a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature with an attack roll, you can attempt to critically injure it. The target makes a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Charisma modifier and {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, the target suffers the following effects:

items.name: Maimed
entries: If the target takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, it can make only one attack.

items.name: Sluggish
entries: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved, and it has a −2 penalty to its {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.
These effects last until the target regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Once a target fails its saving throw against this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Archer
entries: An Arcane Archer studies a unique elven method of archery that weaves magic into attacks to produce supernatural effects. The first Arcane Archers stood watch over their communities and used magic-infused arrows to defeat monsters and invaders. Over the centuries, other species have learned this method of blending magical aptitude with archery. These archers have broadened this method's application to ranged weapons of many kinds and to aspects of adventuring life outside of combat.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Shot
entries: You learn to unleash special magical effects with your shots.

entries.name: Arcane Shot Options
entries: You learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice from the "{@subclassFeature Arcane Shot Options|Fighter|XPHB|Arcane Archer|XUA2025ArcaneUpdates|3|XUA2025ArcaneUpdates}" section later in this subclass's description.

You learn an additional Arcane Shot option of your choice when you reach Fighter levels 7, 10, 15, and 18. Each time you learn a new Arcane Shot option, you can also replace one option you know with a different one.

entries.name: Using Arcane Shot
entries: Once per turn when you make a ranged attack using a weapon with the {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} property, you can apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that attack. You decide to use the option when you hit a creature and deal damage to it unless the option doesn't involve an attack roll.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Arcane Shot Die
entries: Arcane Shot options refer to your Arcane Shot Die. Your Arcane Shot Die is a {@dice d6}.

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: If an Arcane Shot option requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Intelligence modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: At Higher Levels
entries: Your Arcane Shot Die changes when you reach certain {@class Fighter|XPHB} levels. The die becomes a {@dice d8} at level 10, a {@dice d10} at level 15, and a {@dice d12} at level 18.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Shot Options
entries: The Arcane Shot options are presented here in alphabetical order.

subclassFeature.name: Curving Shot
entries: You learn how to direct an errant shot toward a new target. If you make an attack roll with a weapon with the {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} property and miss, you can cause the shot to ricochet toward a new target as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} immediately after the attack misses. The new target must be a creature you can see within the weapon's range and within 60 feet of the attack's original target. Make an attack roll against the new target.

subclassFeature.name: Magical Ammunition
entries: You learn to imbue your ammunition with magical properties. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can imbue a piece of nonmagical ammunition with one of the following magical properties and fire it at a solid surface you can see within the weapon's range. On a hit, the ammunition's effect activates, and the ammunition attaches to the surface it hit for the duration of the effect; when the effect ends, the ammunition is destroyed.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of this feature by expending a use of your {@classFeature Second Wind|Fighter|XPHB|1|XPHB} (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Darkening Shot
entries: Magical shadows fill a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the ammunition for 1 minute. Nonmagical flames in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} are extinguished, and creatures in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} have a −5 penalty to Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks and {@variantrule Passive Perception|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Unlocking Shot
entries: A burst of magic fills a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the ammunition. A loud knock, audible up to 300 feet away, also emits from the ammunition. Any object in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} that is held shut by a mundane lock or that is stuck or barred becomes unlocked, unstuck, or unbarred. If the object has multiple locks, only one of them is unlocked.

subclassFeature.name: Vine Shot
entries: A 60-foot-long vine grows from the ammunition. You and other creatures can then climb it. The vine withers away after 10 minutes.

subclassFeature.name: Ever-Ready Shot
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can regain one expended use of Arcane Shot.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Burst
entries: You can push creatures away from you with your arcane mastery. When you use {@classFeature Indomitable|Fighter|XPHB|9|XPHB}, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your Arcane Shot DC or be pushed up to 20 feet straight away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Masterful Shots
entries: You employ agility in your sharpshooting When a creature you can see misses you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} away from the attacker without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. You can then make a ranged attack roll against the attacker as part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} if the attacker is within the weapon's range.

subclassFeature.name: Tattooed Warrior
entries: Drawing from body-marking traditions from across the multiverse, Tattooed Warriors can use arcane power imbued in their magic tattoos. These Monks gain tattoos as they grow in martial prowess and insight. Tattooed Warriors can reshape their tattoos to access a suite of physical and magical effects.

subclassFeature.name: Beast Tattoos
entries: You gain two animal tattoos. Choose two tattoos from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Tattoos
entries: You gain the magic tattoos described by other features of this subclass. The tattoos appear on your body wherever you wish. Damage or injury doesn't impair your magic tattoos' function. A magic tattoo can look like a brand, scarification, a birthmark, patterns of scales, or any other cosmetic alteration.

If a tattoo's effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Wisdom modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Your spellcasting ability for spells granted by a tattoo is Wisdom.
Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can reshape one of your magic tattoos, changing the option you chose from one list to another option on the same list.

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Tattoo
entries: You gain an additional magic tattoo depicting a celestial phenomenon. Choose a tattoo from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Nature Tattoo
entries: You gain an additional magic tattoo depicting a natural feature. Choose a tattoo from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Monster Tattoo
entries: You gain a magic tattoo depicting a mighty creature. Choose a tattoo from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Conjurer
entries: You consider distance and matter to be flexible guidelines rather than physical laws. Conjurers harness the power of magic that moves creatures instantly through space and summons creatures to fight on their behalf.

subclassFeature.name: Benign Transposition
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. Alternatively, you can choose a space within range that is occupied by a Medium or smaller creature. If that creature is willing, you both teleport, swapping places.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Distant Transposition
entries: The range of your {@subclassFeature Benign Transposition|Wizard|XPHB|Conjurer|XUA2025ArcaneUpdates|3|XUA2025ArcaneUpdates} feature increases to 60 feet. Additionally, you now regain all expended uses of it when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Durable Summons
entries: When you cast a Conjuration spell to summon or create a creature using a spell slot, that creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Wizard level when it first appears. While it has these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, the creature has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant.

subclassFeature.name: Splintered Summons
entries: When you cast {@spell Summon Aberration|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Construct|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Dragon|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Elemental|XPHB}, or {@spell Summon Fey|XPHB} using a spell slot, you can modify the spell to summon two creatures with the spell instead of one. Each creature is of the same kind, uses the stat block and rules denoted by the spell, and manifests in a different unoccupied space of your choice within the spell's range, but the summoned creatures' {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are halved. If you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell, both creatures disappear.

Once you use this feature to modify a spell in this way, you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you do so again. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Enchanter
entries: Your magic clouds or captivates minds. Some Enchanters use their abilities to encourage peace and soften cruelty, while others use their mind-altering magic for selfish ends. Many Enchanters fall somewhere in between.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanting Conversationalist
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice:  {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, or {@skill Persuasion}.
In addition, when you make an ability check with the chosen skill, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

subclassFeature.name: Hypnotic Presence
entries: Your charming words and enchanting gaze can enthrall another creature. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose one creature that you can see within 10 feet of yourself. If the target can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 1 minute or until your {@status Concentration|XPHB} ends, the target is more than 10 feet away from you, the target can neither see nor hear you, or the target takes damage. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this way, the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 1+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Split Enchantment
entries: When you cast an Enchantment spell, such as {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, increase the spell's effective level by 1.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Instinctive Charm
entries: When a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attack misses instead, and if there is another creature within range of the attack other than the attacker, the attacker targets that creature with the triggering attack, using the same attack roll. If multiple creatures are within the attack's range, you choose which one to target.

Once you use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by casting an Enchantment spell with a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Alter Memories
entries: You can reliably enchant creatures and alter their memories. You always have the {@spell Modify Memory|XPHB} spell prepared. When you cast the spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within range of the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Necromancer
entries: You explore the cosmic forces of life, death, and undeath. As a Necromancer, you learn to manipulate the energy that animates all creatures. As you progress, you learn to use magic to drain the life force from a creature and transform its vital energy into magical power. Many see Necromancers as menacing or villainous, though not all Necromancers are evil. Nevertheless, the manipulation of life and death is considered taboo in many societies.

subclassFeature.name: Necromancy Spellbook
entries: The necromantic secrets in your spellbook grant you additional powers. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Necrotic Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Grim Harvest
entries: When you cast a Necromancy spell using a spell slot, you can choose an Undead creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself to regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the level of the spell slot expended plus your Wizard level.

entries.name: Undead Familiar
entries: The {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell appears in your spellbook. When you cast the spell, you choose one of the normal forms for your familiar or one of the following special forms: {@creature Skeleton|XMM} or {@creature Zombie|XMM} (see {@book appendix B|XPHB|9} of the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB} for the familiar's stat block).

subclassFeature.name: Undead Thralls
entries: You always have {@spell Animate Dead|XPHB} prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot. Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that the spell's effective level is increased by 1.
In addition, whenever you cast a Necromancy spell using a spell slot that creates or summons an Undead, the Undead gains the benefits below.

entries.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: The Undead's Hit Point maximum and current {@variantrule Hit points|XPHB} increase by a number equal to the level of the spell slot used plus your Intelligence modifier for the duration of the spell.

entries.name: Withering Strike
entries: Whenever the Undead hits a creature with an attack roll, the Undead deals extra Necrotic damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1 Necrotic damage).

subclassFeature.name: Harvest Undead
entries: You have learned more secrets about the nuances of life and death. Immediately after you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB} but aren't killed outright from taking damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce an Undead creature under your control that you can see to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. You then immediately regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wizard level.

subclassFeature.name: Death's Master
entries: Abstruse rituals within your spellbook allow you mastery over forces of death. While holding your spellbook, you gain the following benefits.

subclassFeature.name: Extinguish Undead
entries: When an Undead creature you can see is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can cause it to explode with necrotic energy. Roll a number of {@dice d6|d6s} equal to half the creature's unexpended {@variantrule Hit Dice|XPHB} (round up, minimum of {@dice 1d6}) and add them together. Each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Undead makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes Necrotic damage equal to the number rolled and can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only. When you use this feature to explode an Undead creature you don't control, you must take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend a level 5+ spell slot to do so.

subclassFeature.name: Transmuter
entries: You study spells that modify energy and matter. To you, the world is not a fixed thing, but eminently mutable. Your magic gives you the tools to become a smith at reality's forge.
Some Transmuters are tinkerers and pranksters. Others pursue their magical studies with deadly seriousness, seeking the power make and destroy worlds.

subclassFeature.name: Transmuter's Stone
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create a magic stone that lasts until you use this feature again. The stone is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Wizard spells. A creature with the stone in its possession gains proficiency in Constitution saving throws and one of the following benefits, which you choose when you create the stone. You can change the stone's benefit when you cast a Transmutation spell using a spell slot.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: The bearer gains {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet or increases the range of its {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} by 60 feet.

entries.name: Speed
entries: The bearer's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.

entries.name: Resistance
entries: The bearer gains Resistance to Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder damage (your choice each time you choose this benefit).

subclassFeature.name: Wondrous Enhancement
entries: You always have the {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
While under the effects of {@spell Alter Self|XPHB}, you gain an additional benefit for each of its options.

entries.name: Aquatic Adaptation
entries: While underwater, you can take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Change Appearance
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: The damage of your new growth increases to {@damage 2d6} damage of the type associated with the growth. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Transmutation
entries: When you cast a Transmutation spell that doesn't deal damage, such as {@spell Fly|XPHB} or {@spell Magical Weapon|XPHB}, using a spell slot, you can treat the spell as if it were cast using a spell slot 1 level above the slot expended.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Stone
entries: Your Transmuter's Stone is more versatile. When you create your Transmuter's Stone, you can choose up to two benefits. You can choose each option other than Resistance only once. If you choose Resistance twice, you must choose different damage types. You can change either or both benefits when you cast a Transmutation spell using a spell slot.
In addition, the following are now among your benefit options for Transmuter's Stone.

entries.name: Mighty Build
entries: The bearer has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength saving throws. The bearer also counts as one size larger when determining its carrying capacity.

entries.name: Tremorsense
entries: The bearer gains {@sense Tremorsense|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Shapechanger
entries: You always have the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

In addition, when you target yourself with the spell, you can modify the spell to gain the benefits below. Once you modify the spell using this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Game Statistics
entries: You retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak. You also retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats.

entries.name: Transmute Spells
entries: You can cast Transmutation spells while shape-shifted, except for any spell with a Material component that has a cost specified or is consumed by the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Master Transmuter
entries: While you carry your Transmuter's Stone, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to consume the reserve of transmutation magic stored inside and choose one of the following benefits. After you use your Transmuter's Stone in this way, the stone crumbles to dust. You can prevent the stone from crumbling by expending a level 5+ spell slot as part of the {@action Magic|XPHB} action you take using this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Major Transformation
entries: You can transmute one nonmagical object—no larger than a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} or eight connected 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes}—into another nonmagical object of similar size and mass and of equal or lesser value. You must spend 10 minutes handling the object to transform it.

subclassFeature.name: Panacea
entries: You touch a creature as part of this {@action Magic|XPHB} action, and the target regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half its Hit Point maximum (round down). The target is cured of all magical contagions, and any curses affecting the target are lifted, including the target's {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to a cursed item. If the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} or {@condition Petrified|XPHB} conditions, those conditions end.

subclassFeature.name: Restore Life
entries: You cast the {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot, using the stone in place of the required Material components.

subclassFeature.name: Restore Youth
entries: You touch one willing creature as part of this {@action Magic|XPHB} action, and the target's {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, if any, decreases to 0 and it permanently appears {@dice 3d10} years younger, to a minimum of young adulthood.

subclassFeature.name: Hollow Warden
entries: Legends tell that the most ancient and fearsome terrors lurk deep within the old places of the earth—unfathomable and bloodthirsty entities of fallow soil and sharpened claws. Hollow Wardens venerate and draw power from such beings, transforming themselves into similarly merciless and monstrous guardians that stalk along jagged coastlines, across steep mountain crags, and through other dark and wild places.

subclassFeature.name: Hollow Warden Spells
entries: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the {@table Hollow Warden Spells|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} table, you thereafter always have the listed spell prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Wrath of the Wild
entries: You draw on the strange and ancient horrors of the land for power. When you cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB}, you transform, gaining the following benefits for the duration of the spell.

entries.name: Ancient Armor
entries: You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1). Your body is wreathed in rotten bark or beastly bristles.

entries.name: Unnerving Aura
entries: When an enemy starts its turn within a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, it makes a Wisdom saving throw against {@dcYourSpellSave}. On a failed save, it can take either an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on this turn, not both. Your shadow lengthens and twists around you, or you sprout unnatural growths like bloody antlers or putrid fangs.

subclassFeature.name: Hungering Might
entries: You gain a bonus to Constitution saving throws equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

In addition, once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll while you are transformed using {@subclassFeature Wrath of the Wild|Ranger|XPHB|Hollow Warden|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}, you regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Wisdom modifier, provided you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB} when you hit.

subclassFeature.name: Rot and Violence
entries: Your dedication to eldritch forest beings alters you further. When transformed using {@subclassFeature Wrath of the Wild|Ranger|XPHB|Hollow Warden|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}, you gain the following additional benefits.

entries.name: Eerie Aura
entries: When a creature fails its saving throw against your {@subclassFeature Wrath of the Wild|Ranger|XPHB|Hollow Warden|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|Unnerving Aura}, it also takes Necrotic, Poison, or Psychic damage (your choice) equal to your Ranger level. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.

entries.name: Strangling Roots
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon, you can activate the {@itemMastery Sap|XPHB} or {@itemMastery Slow|XPHB} mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Ancient Endurance
entries: You become wholly suffused with the forest's ancient power, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Persistent Hunt
entries: If you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while transformed using {@subclassFeature Wrath of the Wild|Ranger|XPHB|Hollow Warden|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} and don't die outright, you can expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required). Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} then instead change to an amount equal to five times the level of the spell slot expended.

entries.name: Timeless
entries: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} condition.

subclassFeature.name: Phantom
entries: Some Rogues walk along the veil between life and death, shepherding opponents to the grave and slipping through the world as undetectable as a spirit. In these pursuits, a Rogue might discover a mystical connection to death itself. Such an individual becomes immersed in negative energy, infusing their strikes with deathly energy and stealing knowledge from souls who have passed on. Thieves' guilds value Rogues of this persuasion as highly effective information gatherers and spies.

subclassFeature.name: Wails from the Grave
entries: Immediately after you deal your {@classFeature Sneak Attack|Rogue|XPHB|1|XPHB} damage to a creature on your turn, you can target a second creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature. Roll half the number of {@classFeature Sneak Attack|Rogue|XPHB|1|XPHB} dice for your level (round up), and the second creature takes Necrotic damage equal to the roll's total as wails of the dead momentarily sound around them.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Whispers of the Dead
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain it, as a ghostly presence shares its knowledge with you. You lose this proficiency when you use this benefit again to choose a different proficiency.

subclassFeature.name: Tokens of the Departed
entries: The spirits of the dead are drawn to you, and echoes of their past lives magically manifest as strange curios with resonant power.

Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain two soul trinkets; each soul trinket is a Tiny object (the DM determines the trinket's form or has you roll on the {@item Trinket|XPHB|Trinkets} table in the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB} to generate it). These trinkets last until you finish another {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, at which point they disintegrate. If you ever move more than 30 feet from a trinket, the trinket immediately teleports to you, appearing somewhere on your person.
The number of soul trinkets you gain when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} increases when you reach Rogue levels 13 (three trinkets) and 17 (four trinkets).

entries.name: Using Soul Trinkets
entries: You can use soul trinkets in the following ways:

entries.name: Regaining Soul Trinkets
entries: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you dies, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to regain one expended soul trinket.

subclassFeature.name: Voice of Death
entries: You can cast {@spell Speak with Dead|XPHB} once without a spell slot, requiring no spell components and using Dexterity as the spellcasting modifier. You regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Death's Knell
entries: When you deal {@classFeature Sneak Attack|Rogue|XPHB|1|XPHB} damage on your turn, you can expend one soul trinket and immediately use {@subclassFeature Wails from the Grave|Rogue|XPHB|Phantom|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} without expending a use of that feature. The expended trinket is destroyed.

subclassFeature.name: Life Essence
entries: While you have at least one soul trinket, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XScreen} and Constitution saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit Query
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expend one soul trinket and immediately cast the {@spell Augury|XPHB} spell, requiring no spell components and using Dexterity as the spellcasting modifier. The expended trinket is destroyed.

subclassFeature.name: Ghost Walk
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you assume a spectral form, gaining the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend and destroy one of your soul trinkets from {@subclassFeature Tokens of the Departed|Rogue|XPHB|Phantom|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|9|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} (no action required) to restore your use of it.

entries.name: Flight
entries: You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 10 feet and can hover.

entries.name: Hazy Form
entries: Attack rolls have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.

entries.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: You can move through occupied spaces as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. If you end your turn in such a space, you take {@damage 1d10} Force damage.

subclassFeature.name: Death's Friend
entries: Your association with death has become so close that you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Death's Lament
entries: When you use your {@subclassFeature Wails from the Grave|Rogue|XPHB|Phantom|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|3|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} feature, you can deal the Necrotic damage to both the first and the second creature.

entries.name: Draw of Death
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you regain one soul trinket for your {@subclassFeature Tokens of the Departed|Rogue|XPHB|Phantom|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|9|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} if you have none remaining.

subclassFeature.name: Reanimator
entries: Reanimators defy the laws of nature in pursuit of gruesome experiment. These grim Artificers stitch together servants from disparate corpses, use foul magic to strengthen the living, and transform the art of necromancy into a terrifying science.

subclassFeature.name: Jolt to Life
entries: When you cast {@spell Spare the Dying|XPHB}, you can modify the spell so that it sends a jolt of electricity through the target, reviving it. The target regains 1 Hit Point, and each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the target makes a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking Lightning damage equal to {@damage 1d4} plus half your Artificer level (round up) on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
You can modify the spell this way a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Reanimated Companion
entries: Using {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} or another type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you have proficiency, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to create a {@creature Reanimated Companion|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} {@homebrew |(see the stat block)} through the power of necromancy and science. The companion manifests in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. You determine the companion's appearance; your choices don't affect the companion's game statistics.

The companion is {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and your allies and obeys you. It lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} or until you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to dismiss it early, at which point it harmlessly collapses into a pile of viscera. It immediately drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and dies (triggering its Death Burst trait) if you die.

Once you create a companion, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} or expend a spell slot to create one. You can have only one companion at a time and can't create one while your companion is present.

entries.name: The Companion in Combat
entries: In combat, the companion acts during your turn. It can move and take its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHb} to command it to take an action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the companion acts on its own and isn't limited to the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.

subclassFeature.name: Reanimator Spells
entries: When you reach an Artificer level specified in the {@table Reanimator Spells|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Strange Modifications
entries: Whenever you create a {@creature Reanimated Companion|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}, it gains one of the following options of your choice; choose when you create the companion.

entries.name: Arcane Conduit
entries: You can cast spells as though you were in the companion's space, but you must use your own senses. Once per turn, when you cast {@filter an Artificer spell from the Evocation or Necromancy schools|spells|class=artificer|school=v;n} and deal damage while your companion is within 120 feet of you, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

entries.name: Ferocity
entries: When you command your companion to take the Dreadful Swipe action, the companion can use it twice.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Reanimation
entries: You experiment and alter your companion further. Whenever you create a {@creature Reanimated Companion|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}, it gains one of the following options of your choice; choose when you create the companion.

entries.name: Bloated
entries: The companion becomes Large or Medium (your choice). Whenever it hits a Large or smaller creature with its Dreadful Swipe action, that creature can also be pushed up to 10 feet away from the companion. Additionally, you can add your Intelligence modifier to the damage dealt by the companion's Death Burst.

entries.name: Gaunt
entries: The companion's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases to 45 feet, and it gains a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. It can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check. In addition, whenever a creature of your choice starts its turn within a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from your companion, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against {@dcYourSpellSave} or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Moist
entries: The companion gains a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. In addition, whenever the companion is hit by an attack roll from a creature within 10 feet of it, the attacker takes Acid damage equal to your Intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Promethean Reanimation
entries: You have mastered the science of revivification, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Facilitated Revival
entries: When you cast {@spell Revivify|XPHB} or {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB}, the cost of any Material components needed for that spell is halved.

entries.name: Improved Companion
entries: The damage of your {@creature Reanimated Companion|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses|Reanimated Companion's} Death Burst increases to {@damage 4d6}. Necrotic damage dealt by your companion ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHb}.

entries.name: Life Transfer
entries: You can siphon the animating magic of your companion to bolster yourself. When you take damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause your {@creature Reanimated Companion|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses} to drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The companion immediately dies (triggering its Death Burst trait), and you regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Artificer level.

subclassFeature.name: Cavalier
entries: Cavaliers excel at mounted combat and guard those in their charge, often protecting their superiors and the weak. A Cavalier is equally at home leading a cavalry charge or exchanging repartees at a state dinner.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency in one the following skills of your choice: {@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}, {@skill History|XPHB}, {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}. Alternatively, you learn one language of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Born to the Saddle
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to avoid falling off your mount. If you fall off your mount and descend no more than 10 feet, you can land on your feet if you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
In addition, mounting or dismounting a creature costs you only 5 feet of movement rather than half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Unwavering Mark
entries: You can menace your foes, foiling their attacks and punishing them for harming others. When you hit a creature with a Melee weapon, you can mark the creature until the end of your next turn. This effect ends early if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you die, or someone else marks the creature.
While it is within 5 feet of you, a creature marked by you has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against creatures other than you.

In addition, if a creature marked by you hits a creature other than you with an attack roll, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the marked creature until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Warding Maneuver
entries: You learn to fend off strikes directed at you, your mount, or other creatures nearby. If you or a creature you can see within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can roll {@dice 1d8} as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} if you're wielding a Melee weapon or a {@item Shield|XPHB}. Roll the die, and add the number rolled to the target's AC against the triggering attack. If the attack still hits, the target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} against the attack's damage.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Hold the Line
entries: Creatures provoke an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} from you when they move 5 feet or more while within your reach. When you hit a creature with an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 until the end of the current turn.

subclassFeature.name: Ferocious Charger
entries: During the first round of each combat, you and your mount's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and your movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} for that round. When you move within 5 feet of a creature this round, that creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@table Proficiency bonus|XPHB}) or you either push it 5 feet away or cause it to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. A creature makes this save only once during a turn.

subclassFeature.name: Vigilant Defender
entries: In combat, you get a special {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} that you can take once on every creature's turn except your turn. You can take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} only to make an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, and you can't take it on the same turn that you take your normal {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Oathbreaker
entries: The power of a Paladin's oath is so great that it holds a distorted power even when it is violated. When a Paladin betrays the tenets of their oath because of corruption, pride, or thirst for power, they lose the blessing granted by their original oath but sometimes take on the sinister blessing of the Oathbreaker in its place. Oathbreakers use fear and overwhelming power to achieve their goals. While many Oathbreakers are beyond redemption, some Paladins bend this oath to achieve a great and noble deed to atone for their past evils.

At the DM's discretion, a Paladin of any level who violates the tenets of their oath can lose the benefits granted by their original subclass and gain the features of the Oathbreaker subclass instead. Alternatively, a Paladin can choose this subclass at level 3 based on the character's backstory of falling from grace.
These Paladins often share the following tenets:

items: Fear is a tool in the hands of the powerful.
Strive for power at any cost.
Do what must be done, but never lose sight of your goal.

subclassFeature.name: Conjure Undead
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Channel Divinity|Paladin|XPHB|3|XPHB} to summon a number of Undead equal to half your Charisma modifier (round up; minimum of one Undead summoned). Each Undead appears in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you and is a type of your choice: {@creature Skeleton|XMM} or {@creature Zombie|XMM} (see {@book appendix B of the Player's Handbook|XPHB|9} for the stat blocks). The Undead are under your control for 1 minute, after which they dissolve into ash.

Each Undead is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the Undead shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

subclassFeature.name: Dreadful Aspect
entries: Immediately after you cast {@spell Divine Smite|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Channel Divinity|Paladin|XPHB|3|XPHB} to channel a burst of magical menace. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. A {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Oathbreaker Spells
entries: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a {@class Paladin|XPHB} level specified in the {@table Oathbreaker Spells|XUA2025SubclassesUpdate} table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Hate
entries: When you, as well as any Fiend or Undead in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB} that is an ally, hits a creature with a melee attack, that attack deals extra Necrotic damage equal to your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Supernatural Resistance
entries: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

subclassFeature.name: Dread Lord
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imbue your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB} with unholy gloom, granting the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required).

items.name: Darkness
entries: Magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} fills your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB}. You and allies in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB} can see within this magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}.

items.name: Fear
entries: Whenever a creature with the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition starts its turn in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB}, that creature takes {@damage 4d10} Psychic damage.

items.name: Shadow Strike
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make a melee spell attack, shaping the gloom exuding from you into a blade that targets one creature in your {@classFeature Aura of Protection|Paladin|XPHB|6|XPHB}. On a hit, the attack deals Necrotic damage equal to {@damage 3d10} plus your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Stealer
entries: While ordinary thieves are cutting purses and burgling homes, a Magic Stealer is after a much more valuable prize: magic. The Magic Stealer preys on spellcasters, repurposing their magic and eluding divination spells to take magical power at the point of a knife.

subclassFeature.name: Drain Magic
entries: You can drain power from ongoing spells to recharge your allies' magic. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} Action, you can touch a willing creature and end one ongoing level 1 or level 2 spell on it; the creature immediately recovers one expended spell slot of level 2 or lower (the target's choice).
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Empower Sneak Attack
entries: Immediately after a creature you can see within 30 feet of you casts a level 1+ spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to absorb magical energy from the spell. When you do so, until the end of your next turn, the next time you hit with your {@classFeature Sneak Attack|Rogue|XPHB|1|PHB} you deal extra Force damage. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of {@dice d6|d6s} equal to the spell's level, and add them together.

You can take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Magical Sabotage
entries: You gain the following Cunning Strike options.

subclassFeature.name: Disrupt Spell (Cost: 3d6)
entries: The target's magical acuity is disrupted until the start of your next turn. Whenever the target casts a spell during that time, it must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or the spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Susceptibility (Cost: 2d6)
entries: The target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes against a spell until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Steal Resistance (Cost: 2d6)
entries: Choose one kind of damage. If the target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that kind of damage, until the start of your next turn, the target loses that {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Drain Magic
entries: You can now use {@subclassFeature Drain Magic|Rogue|XPHB|MAgic Stealer|XUA2026MysticSubclasses|3|XUA2026MysticSubclasses} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. In addition, when you use {@subclassFeature Drain Magic|Rogue|XPHB|MAgic Stealer|XUA2026MysticSubclasses|3|XUA2026MysticSubclasses}, you can end one ongoing level 1, level 2, or level 3 spell on the target, and the target recovers one expended spell slot of level 3 or lower (the target's choice).

subclassFeature.name: Occult Shroud
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can cast the {@spell Nondetection} spell, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. When you do so, you can target only yourself, and the duration increases to 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Eldritch Implosion
entries: When you use {@subclassFeature Empower Sneak Attack|Rogue|XPHB|MAgic Stealer|XUA2026MysticSubclasses|3|XUA2026MysticSubclasses}, you can force the target to make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Dexterity modifier and {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, the spell dissipates with no effect, and the target has the {@condition Stunned} condition until the start of its next turn.
